Name: RicHaRd HEnSlEy
chief_complaint: Severe joint pain and swelling in hands and knees
hpi: A 29-year-old female with a history of arthritis presents to the ED with acute onset of severe polyarticular pain and swelling, primarily affecting her hands and knees. physician_note: A: 29F presenting with an acute arthritis flare, likely related to her underlying inflammatory arthritis. Differential diagnosis includes rheumatoid arthritis exacerbation, psoriatic arthritis, or reactive arthritis given the acute polyarticular nature. Septic arthritis is less likely given the absence of systemic signs of infection. P: Administer oral NSAIDs for symptomatic relief and obtain ESR/CRP to assess inflammatory markers. Will consult rheumatology for potential medication adjustment and discharge with close follow-up.

Name: pAul pereZ
chief_complaint: Severe lower back pain for two days
hpi: Patient is a 48-year-old obese female with a known history of chronic low back pain, typically managed with oral ibuprofen. She presents to the emergency department complaining of an acute exacerbation of severe, sharp lower back pain, radiating to the left gluteal region, which began approximately two days prior. The pain is exacerbated by movement and partially alleviated by rest, with no associated numbness, weakness, fever, or changes in bowel or bladder function.
physician_note: A&P: 48-year-old obese female presenting with acute on chronic low back pain, consistent with a musculoskeletal etiology. Physical exam reveals significant paraspinal tenderness but no neurological deficits, indicating a likely lumbar strain or sciatica flare. Differential diagnoses include lumbar strain, discopathy, or myofascial pain. Plan includes ordering lumbar spine X-rays to rule out bony pathology, administering IV analgesia, and providing discharge instructions with muscle relaxants and follow-up recommendations with her primary care physician for ongoing pain management.

Name: RODNey tRavIS

physician_note: 39 y/o female with metastatic cancer on chemo presenting with generalized weakness and low-grade fever. DDx includes neutropenic fever/sepsis, tumor progression, or chemotherapy-related fatigue/dehydration. Plan: Labs including CBC, CMP, blood cultures, urinalysis. IV fluids, broad-spectrum antibiotics empirically after cultures drawn. Monitor vitals closely, admit for further workup.

physician_note: Patient presents in hypovolemic shock secondary to suspected massive pulmonary embolism given clinical picture, hypotension, and hypoxia. Differential includes acute coronary syndrome and septic shock, but PE is highest on the list due to risk factors and acute presentation. Plan includes immediate tPA administration, initiation of heparin drip, stat CT pulmonary angiography, and critical care consultation for further management.

chief_complaint: Fever, chills, and feeling unwell
hpi: Patient is a 38-year-old female with a history of breast cancer currently undergoing chemotherapy, with her last cycle completed one week ago. She presents to the emergency department with a 24-hour history of subjective fevers, chills, and generalized weakness, accompanied by a sore throat. She denies productive cough, dysuria, or diarrhea. Her planned elective admission for her next chemotherapy cycle is now in question due to these acute symptoms.
physician_note: 38F with breast cancer, s/p recent chemo, presenting with likely neutropenic fever and pharyngitis. Differential includes chemotherapy-induced neutropenic sepsis vs. viral upper respiratory infection. Plan: IV fluids, pan-cultures (blood, urine, throat swab), empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics (e.g., cefepime), and admission for close monitoring and management of febrile neutropenia.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_90445a61
chief_complaint: Bad leg injury after car accident
hpi: 28 y/o male involved in a high-speed motor vehicle collision (MVC) presents with severe right lower extremity pain and deformity. EMS reports an open wound with exposed bone on the anterior aspect of the tibia. Patient is currently on Ibuprofen for chronic arthritis.
physician_note: A: 28 y/o M with open right tibial fracture status post MVC. High concern for contamination and potential for limb-threatening injury. Differential includes other traumatic injuries (e.g., head trauma, internal bleeding) and compartment syndrome. P: Immediate orthopedic consultation for surgical intervention, aggressive pain management, tetanus prophylaxis, broad-spectrum antibiotics, and wound care. Continue close monitoring of neurovascular status.

Name: kRIStY dAvIdSoN
chief_complaint: Teenage girl with severe joint pain and swelling
hpi: A 17-year-old female with a history of juvenile idiopathic arthritis presents with a 3-day history of progressively worsening bilateral knee pain and swelling, accompanied by diffuse myalgias and subjective fever. She reports increased stiffness, especially in the mornings, and has had difficulty ambulating. She denies any recent trauma or new medications.
physician_note: A/P: 17 y/o F with JIA flare vs. septic arthritis vs. reactive arthritis. Patient presents with acute on chronic polyarthralgia and swelling, with effusions in bilateral knees. Labs show borderline inflammatory markers; synovial fluid analysis is pending. Plan: CBC, ESR, CRP, synovial fluid aspiration, and empiric NSAIDs. Consult Rheumatology for further management and potential antibiotic consideration.

Name: jOn mATHEws
chief_complaint: Weak, confused, fever, and chills for a day
hpi: An 80-year-old male presents to the ED with generalized weakness, increased confusion, and subjective fevers and chills for the past 24 hours. Family reports decreased oral intake and lethargy. Patient has a history of arthritis and denies any chest pain, shortness of breath, or focal neurological deficits.
physician_note: A: 80 y.o. male with acute altered mental status and systemic inflammatory response, highly concerning for urosepsis given reported symptoms. Differential includes other infectious etiologies, metabolic derangements, or neurological event. P: Labs drawn including CBC, CMP, lactate, UA with culture. IV fluids initiated. Broad-spectrum antibiotics ordered after blood cultures. Prepare for potential ICU admission if clinical status deteriorates.

Name: jACQuEline deAn
chief_complaint: Sudden chest pain, feels like pressure
hpi: 57 y.o. male with obesity and history of hypertension presents with 3 hours of substernal chest pressure radiating to the left arm. Pain is 6/10, constant, and not relieved by rest. physician_note: A: 57 y.o. male presenting with acute chest pain, concerning for Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS) given risk factors and symptoms. Other differentials include esophageal spasm, pericarditis, or anxiety. P: EKG, serial troponins, CXR, and basic labs. Administer aspirin and nitroglycerin. Cardiology consult pending further workup.

Name: aMaNdA crOsS
chief_complaint: Bad asthma attack, can't breathe
hpi: 15-year-old female with a history of asthma presents to the ED with acute onset shortness of breath and wheezing, worsening over the past 4 hours. Last ED visit for asthma was 6 months ago.
physician_note: Patient presents with acute asthma exacerbation, likely triggered by a viral illness or environmental allergen. Differential includes bronchitis, foreign body aspiration. Exam reveals diffuse wheezing and increased work of breathing. Plan includes albuterol/ipratropium nebulizers, systemic corticosteroids, and close monitoring. Will consider CXR if no improvement.

Name: RaChel AdKinS
chief_complaint: Severe shortness of breath, can't catch my breath
hpi: 80-year-old male with a history of asthma presents with acute onset and worsening shortness of breath for the past 24 hours. He denies fever, chills, or chest pain, but states he feels too weak to get out of bed.
physician_note: Patient is an elderly male presenting with acute respiratory distress likely secondary to a severe asthma exacerbation. Differential diagnoses include pneumonia, acute decompensated heart failure, and pulmonary embolism given his age and symptoms. Plan involves immediate oxygen therapy, nebulized bronchodilators, systemic corticosteroids, chest X-ray, ECG, and comprehensive lab work to rule out infectious etiology and cardiac involvement.

Name: aNgeL JOHNsON
chief_complaint: Dizziness and generalized weakness
hpi: Patient is a 72-year-old female with a history of hypertension, presenting with a 2-day history of progressive dizziness and generalized weakness. She reports lightheadedness upon standing but denies syncope or recent falls. No associated chest pain, shortness of breath, or focal neurological deficits. physician_note: A: 72F with chronic hypertension presenting with acute onset dizziness and weakness, suggestive of orthostatic intolerance. Differential includes dehydration, medication side effect, cardiac arrhythmia, or anemia. P: Will initiate IV fluid bolus, obtain 12-lead EKG, and order comprehensive labs including CBC, BMP, and cardiac enzymes. Monitor vital signs closely and consider adjusting current medications after further workup.

Name: sAmUel enGlISh
chief_complaint: Feeling off, blood sugar high
hpi: This 24-year-old male with a history of Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus presents with a two-day history of general malaise and feeling unwell. Patient is compliant with his insulin regimen and Lipitor.
physician_note: A: 24M known T1DM presenting with uncomplicated hyperglycemia and vague malaise. No clinical signs of DKA, active infection, or acute cardiac event. Likely a mild viral syndrome or dietary indiscretion affecting glucose control. P: Labs (CBC, BMP, UA) sent, IVF initiated. Insulin regimen to be reviewed and adjusted based on lab results and clinical response. Differential includes viral illness, medication non-adherence, or diet-induced hyperglycemia. Plan for discharge once glucose is controlled and patient is stable with appropriate education.

Name: BrittanY jamEs
chief_complaint: Sudden onset chest pain radiating to arm
hpi: A 46-year-old male with a history of hypertension, currently on aspirin, presents to the ED complaining of sudden onset substernal chest pain for the past 2 hours. physician_note: Assessment: 46 y/o male presenting with acute chest pain concerning for acute coronary syndrome (ACS) given classic symptoms and elevated troponin. Differential diagnoses include pericarditis, aortic dissection, and esophageal spasm. Plan: Patient admitted to cardiology service for further management of NSTEMI vs unstable angina. Continue aspirin, initiate heparin drip, and schedule immediate cardiac catheterization.

Name: eLIzABETh cAsTILlO

Plan to admit for BP control, neuroimaging, and workup for secondary causes of hypertension.

Name: eRika youNg
chief_complaint: Bad asthma attack, can't breathe
hpi: The 17-year-old female, with a known history of asthma, presents to the ED with severe shortness of breath and wheezing, which started approximately 6 hours ago. physician_note: A/P: Severe acute asthma exacerbation. Differentials include viral tracheobronchitis or foreign body aspiration, though less likely given history. Plan: Aggressive bronchodilator therapy with continuous nebulized albuterol and ipratropium, intravenous steroids, and oxygen to maintain SpO2 >92%. Will monitor for response and consider magnesium sulfate if no improvement.

Name: SAra cRoSs

hpi: A 74-year-old female with a history of type 2 diabetes presents to the ED complaining of generalized weakness, increased thirst, and frequent urination for the past three days. Plan includes aggressive IV fluid resuscitation, stat lab work (CBC, BMP, VBG, urinalysis), continuous cardiac monitoring, and preparation for insulin drip initiation pending lab results. Will consult endocrinology as needed.

Name: davID WaRD

hpi: Patient is a 59-year-old male presenting with mild, intermittent chest discomfort for the past 24 hours. physician_note: A&P: 59M presenting with atypical chest pain. Differential includes musculoskeletal pain, gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), and less likely, stable angina given the patient's stable presentation and lack of acute symptoms. Plan: Obtain ECG, order cardiac enzymes including troponin, and a basic metabolic panel. Will monitor patient's symptoms and vital signs closely. If studies are unremarkable and symptoms resolve, patient will be discharged with return precautions and outpatient follow-up.

Name: brIaN fReNCh
chief_complaint: High blood sugar, feeling very weak
hpi: A 25-year-old male with a history of type 1 diabetes presents to the ED reporting generalized weakness, polyuria, polydipsia, and nausea for the past 24 hours. physician_note: 25 y.o. male with T1DM presenting with DKA. Patient is significantly dehydrated and acidotic, likely secondary to insulin non-compliance. Differential includes severe hyperglycemia, DKA precipitated by infection, or other metabolic derangement. Plan is aggressive IV fluids, insulin drip, serial labs including BMP, VBG, and lactate, and close monitoring in the ED with possible ICU admission.

Name: jOSEPh MeNDOZa
chief_complaint: Confused and weak, feeling feverish
hpi: An 80-year-old female presents to the ED with her daughter reporting acute onset of confusion and generalized weakness over the past 24 hours. physician_note: A: 80 y.o. female with acute altered mental status and generalized weakness, likely secondary to an infectious process such as urosepsis or pneumonia, given vital sign abnormalities and initial lab findings of leukocytosis and elevated lactate. Other considerations include metabolic derangement or CNS event. P: Admit to medical service for further workup and management. Initiate empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics (e.g., Ceftriaxone 1g IV once allergy to penicillin is ruled out or alternative if confirmed), IV fluids, and obtain urine/blood cultures.

Name: KYLe huMpHReY

physician_note: Assessment: 47 y.o. male with known T2DM presenting with symptomatic hyperglycemia. Patient is alert and oriented, without signs of DKA or HHS. Differential includes medication non-adherence, dietary indiscretion, or underlying infection. Plan: Obtain full metabolic panel, A1c, and urinalysis. Administer subcutaneous insulin and oral rehydration. Provide diabetic education.

Name: aLan ShARp
chief_complaint: Weakness, dizziness, and trouble breathing
hpi: An 83-year-old obese male presents with a 2-day history of increasing generalized weakness, fatigue, and occasional dizziness upon standing. physician_note: 83 yo M with acute onset weakness, dyspnea, and mild confusion. Differential includes UTI, early sepsis, cardiac arrhythmia/decompensation, or dehydration. Plan includes IV fluids, full septic workup (UA, blood cultures, CXR), EKG, and comprehensive metabolic panel. Monitor vitals closely for any deterioration.

Name: cLareNCE evaNs

physician_note: A&P: 75F with acute asthma exacerbation. Differential includes CHF, pneumonia, and cardiac ischemia given her age and aspirin use. Plan: Nebulized bronchodilators, systemic corticosteroids, oxygen therapy, EKG, CXR, and labs including cardiac enzymes to rule out other etiologies. Close monitoring for respiratory decompensation.

Name: JOsePH belL
chief_complaint: Stomach pain and dark stools
hpi: 32 y/o female with a history of arthritis on daily aspirin presents with 2 days of worsening generalized abdominal pain, nausea, and some dark, tarry stools. She denies frank hematemesis or melena. Pain is dull, 6/10, and has been intermittent. She reports increased fatigue.
physician_note: A: 32 y/o F with history of arthritis and aspirin use presenting with abdominal pain and melena, concerning for upper GI bleed or severe gastritis. Differential includes peptic ulcer disease, esophagitis, or medication-induced gastritis. Initial labs show borderline WBC count. P: IV fluids, pantoprazole IV, stool guaiac, type and screen. Consult GI for possible endoscopy and monitor serial Hgb/Hct.

Name: JUsTin MoyER

Assessment is likely dehydration and electrolyte imbalance secondary to chemotherapy side effects, with a differential including infection, disease progression, or GI bleed given Ibuprofen use. Plan includes IV fluids, comprehensive labs including CBC, CMP, lactate, and Troponin, and consideration of CT imaging pending lab results. Will monitor vitals and symptoms closely.

Name: aArOn LOPEz
chief_complaint: Severe shortness of breath, can't catch my breath

physician_note: Elderly asthmatic presenting in acute respiratory distress, likely severe asthma exacerbation with possible superimposed pneumonia given cough and malaise. Differential also includes acute decompensated heart failure. Plan: Nebulized bronchodilators, systemic steroids, oxygen therapy, broad-spectrum antibiotics, and further workup including CXR and comprehensive labs.

Name: JessICA CHuNG
chief_complaint: Teenager with sudden chest pain
hpi: A 13-year-old obese male presents with acute onset substernal chest pain, described as sharp and non-radiating, 7/10 in intensity, starting 2 hours prior. He denies recent trauma or fever. physician_note: 13 yo obese male presenting with acute chest pain. Differential includes musculoskeletal strain, esophagitis, anxiety, but cardiac etiology must be ruled out given the atypical presentation for age and aspirin use. Plan: EKG, cardiac enzymes, CXR, and observation. Will trend troponins and consult pediatric cardiology if indicated.

Name: JUlIan joHNsOn

hpi: 21-year-old female with a history of acute lymphoblastic leukemia, currently undergoing chemotherapy, presents with increasing generalized fatigue and weakness over the past 5 days. physician_note: A&P: 21F with ALL post-chemo presenting with worsening fatigue. Differential includes myelosuppression, electrolyte imbalance, or early infection, though afebrile on presentation. Plan: CBC, CMP, blood cultures, consider CXR if any respiratory symptoms develop. Administer IV fluids for hydration and manage symptoms. Discuss with oncology for further guidance.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_be66592d

hpi: A 32-year-old male presents after falling onto an outstretched right hand earlier today. He reports immediate onset of severe pain, swelling, and limited range of motion in the right wrist. Neurovascular status distally is intact. Plan includes plain film radiography of the wrist to confirm fracture, pain management with oral analgesics, and orthopedic consultation for definitive treatment and splinting.

Name: jAsoN leOnARd
chief_complaint: Severe asthma attack, can't catch breath
hpi: A 48-year-old male with a history of persistent asthma presents to the emergency department with acute onset of severe shortness of breath and wheezing, worsening over the last 12 hours. Plan: Initiate continuous nebulized albuterol and ipratropium, administer IV methylprednisolone, and obtain STAT chest X-ray and EKG. Monitor oxygen saturation and respiratory effort closely.

Name: jAmIE PoOLe

Plan: Will obtain abdominal ultrasound to further evaluate for acute pathology. Continue with IV fluids and pain management. Will consult GI if symptoms persist or worsen after initial imaging.

Name: mIchaeL PARKer

physician_note: A 53-year-old obese male with acute epigastric pain concerning for pancreatitis, peptic ulcer disease, or cholecystitis. Initial labs are significant for leukocytosis and elevated glucose. Plan includes NPO, IV fluids, pain control, and further imaging with CT abdomen/pelvis. Consult GI if symptoms persist. Patient will be admitted for further management and observation.

Name: AusTiN hERnanDeZ

Name: DEaNnA pALMeR

physician_note: A/P: 13-year-old male with acute abdominal pain concerning for appendicitis, gastroenteritis, or less likely, cholecystitis given obesity. Patient appears uncomfortable but not in extremis. Plan: IV fluids, antiemetics, CBC, CMP, lipase, urinalysis, abdominal ultrasound, and surgical consultation for evaluation of acute abdomen. Monitor for worsening symptoms.

Name: jessICa pINEDA

hpi: This 15-year-old male with a history of acute lymphoblastic leukemia, currently on maintenance chemotherapy, presents for routine follow-up and evaluation of mild, generalized body aches over the past 2 days. physician_note: A&P: 15 y.o. male with ALL on maintenance chemo presenting with mild myalgias. Vitals stable, physical exam benign. Differential includes chemotherapy-related myalgias, mild viral syndrome, or anxiety. Plan: Continue home ibuprofen, reinforce return precautions for fever or worsening symptoms, and continue outpatient oncology follow-up as scheduled.

Name: JEsSIca KElLer
chief_complaint: Severe headache and blurry vision
hpi: Patient is a 31-year-old male with a history of hypertension, currently on Lipitor, presenting with a severe headache and blurry vision for the past 6 hours. physician_note: A&P: 31 y.o. male with known HTN presenting with hypertensive urgency/emergency given severe headache and blurry vision with significantly elevated BP. Plan: Initiate IV antihypertensives (Labetalol), obtain ECG, head CT to rule out intracranial pathology, and comprehensive lab panel including CBC, CMP, and cardiac enzymes. Name: jamEs BranDT

physician_note: A&P: 75 y/o male with acute right hip pain post-fall. Concerns for hip fracture versus severe contusion or acute arthritis exacerbation. Patient is on Lipitor. Plan includes X-rays of the right hip, pain management, and orthopedic consultation. Will monitor neurovascular status of the extremity.

Name: erin hIlL
chief_complaint: Difficulty breathing and wheezing for two days
hpi: Patient is a 57-year-old male with a history of asthma presenting with increased shortness of breath and wheezing for the past 48 hours. He reports using his albuterol inhaler more frequently than usual, but with diminishing relief. Denies fever, cough, chest pain, or recent illness exposure.
physician_note: Assessment: 57-year-old male with acute asthma exacerbation. Patient presents with classic symptoms of wheezing and dyspnea, responding partially to rescue inhaler use. Differential includes acute bronchitis, COPD exacerbation, and cardiac etiology. Plan: Initiate bronchodilator nebulizer treatments and systemic corticosteroids. Monitor oxygen saturation and respiratory effort; consider chest X-ray if no improvement or suspected pneumonia.

Name: RIChArD LaWSon
chief_complaint: Severe chest pain and can't catch my breath.

Patient will be admitted for further workup and continuous cardiac monitoring. Will also consider a CT angiogram of the chest if PE risk remains high.

Name: Amanda cHAPMaN

hpi: A 27-year-old obese female presents to the ED with acute onset, severe epigastric pain that has migrated to the right upper quadrant over the past 8 hours. Name: josHuA MORRiS

hpi: A 26 y/o male with a history of obesity presents with acute onset substernal chest pain radiating to the left arm, associated with dyspnea. Symptoms began approximately 2 hours prior to arrival, unrelieved by rest. He endorses diaphoresis but denies recent trauma or fever.
physician_note: 26 y/o male with obesity presenting with acute chest pain concerning for acute coronary syndrome given symptoms and risk factors. Other considerations include pulmonary embolism, pericarditis, or severe GERD. Plan is to initiate cardiac workup including serial troponins, EKG, chest X-ray. Administer aspirin, nitrates, and oxygen as needed. Consult cardiology for further management.

Name: CYntHIA MCClain

physician_note: Pt is a 76 y/o male with LLQ pain. Differential includes diverticulitis, perforated viscus, ischemic colitis, or GI bleed. Labs show leukocytosis and hyperglycemia. Plan: IV fluids, broad-spectrum antibiotics, pain control, NPO, surgical consult, and CT abdomen/pelvis.

Name: natHAN white

physician_note: 27 yo male with acute epigastric pain concerning for pancreatitis vs. cholecystitis, especially given history of obesity and hyperlipidemia on Lipitor. Will admit for further management if imaging or labs are concerning, or for pain control.

Name: mR. josEPh HolmeS DdS

hpi: 55-year-old male with history of osteoarthritis and on Lipitor presents with acute onset substernal chest pain radiating to the left arm, associated with dyspnea and diaphoresis. physician_note: A: 55 y.o. male presenting with acute chest pain concerning for acute coronary syndrome. Differential includes myocardial infarction, pulmonary embolism, or esophageal spasm. P: Initiate cardiac workup including serial ECGs, cardiac enzymes, and aspirin. IV access, O2 as needed, pain control, and prepare for potential cath lab activation.

Name: Tara kIdD

hpi: A 19-year-old male with a history of hypertension presents with acute onset substernal chest pain radiating to his left arm, starting approximately 2 hours ago. Name: maRy rIVera
chief_complaint: Severe shortness of breath and wheezing
hpi: A 31-year-old male with a history of asthma presents to the ED with acute worsening of dyspnea and wheezing for the past 24 hours. He reports increased cough and chest tightness, which has not improved with frequent albuterol inhaler use. Patient denies fever or recent illness but notes increased environmental allergens.
physician_note: Patient presenting with acute asthma exacerbation, likely triggered by environmental factors. Differential diagnosis includes viral bronchitis or allergic reaction. Plan: Administer nebulized albuterol/ipratropium, initiate systemic corticosteroids, obtain a chest X-ray to rule out pneumonia, and monitor respiratory status closely. Consider magnesium sulfate if no significant improvement.

Name: KelLIE FrAnKlin
chief_complaint: Can't catch my breath, asthma acting up
hpi: 65-year-old female with a history of asthma presents to the ED with acute onset of progressively worsening shortness of breath, wheezing, and chest tightness for the past 6 hours. She reports using her albuterol inhaler every 2 hours with only transient relief. Denies fever, cough, or recent illness.
physician_note: A: Acute asthma exacerbation. Differential includes bronchitis or early pneumonia. P: Administer albuterol/ipratropium nebulizer, oral corticosteroids (prednisone), and monitor respiratory status. Will reassess after initial treatments and consider chest X-ray if no improvement.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_c19768bd
chief_complaint: Fever and severe belly pain
hpi: 61-year-old male with a history of colon cancer currently undergoing chemotherapy presents to the ED with a 2-day history of subjective fevers and worsening diffuse abdominal pain. He denies nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, or urinary symptoms. Last chemotherapy dose was 5 days ago.
physician_note: A&P: 61M with colon cancer on chemo presenting with neutropenic fever and acute abdominal pain. High concern for typhlitis, sepsis, or C. difficile colitis. Plan to admit for aggressive IV fluids, broad-spectrum antibiotics, STAT CBC, CMP, lactate, blood cultures, and CT abdomen/pelvis.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_fb006974
chief_complaint: Severe tearing chest pain with nausea and sweats
hpi: A 62-year-old male with a history of hypertension and on daily aspirin presents with acute onset severe, tearing chest pain radiating to his left arm, starting approximately 2 hours ago. He reports associated nausea, profuse diaphoresis, and shortness of breath. The pain is unrelenting and rated 9/10 in intensity.
physician_note: Assessment: 62M presenting with acute chest pain highly concerning for STEMI given the tearing quality, radiation, and associated symptoms including diaphoresis. Differential includes aortic dissection, pulmonary embolism, and acute pericarditis. Plan: Immediate ECG, cardiac enzymes, IV access, oxygen, aspirin (if not already taken), nitrates if BP allows, and activate cath lab given high suspicion.

Name: fRedErICk ESpINoZa
chief_complaint: Severe difficulty breathing, asthma attack
hpi: A 25-year-old male with a history of asthma presents to the emergency department with progressively worsening shortness of breath over the past 24 hours. He reports increased wheezing and coughing, with minimal relief from his home albuterol inhaler. He denies fever or chills but feels significantly more distressed than usual.
physician_note: A&P: This is a 25-year-old male with an acute severe asthma exacerbation, likely triggered by a viral illness given mild temperature elevation. Differential includes pneumonia, pneumothorax, or acute bronchitis. Plan involves immediate nebulized bronchodilators, systemic corticosteroids, oxygen therapy, and close monitoring. Chest X-ray and labs including CBC to rule out infection and assess severity. Admission is likely required.

Name: NIchOlas RiCHArDSOn
chief_complaint: Severe worsening belly pain
hpi: A 16-year-old obese male presents to the ED with 12 hours of worsening periumbilical abdominal pain that has migrated to the right lower quadrant. He reports associated nausea and one episode of non-bloody, non-bilious emesis. Patient states he took ibuprofen without relief prior to arrival.
physician_note: 16 y/o obese male with acute RLQ pain concerning for appendicitis. Differential diagnoses include mesenteric adenitis or gastroenteritis. Plan includes CBC, CMP, urinalysis, abdominal ultrasound, IV fluids, pain control, NPO status, and surgical consult.

Name: juStin keLley
chief_complaint: Chest discomfort and shortness of breath
hpi: A 22-year-old obese female presents to the emergency department with a 6-hour history of intermittent, non-radiating chest discomfort described as a dull ache. She reports the pain is exacerbated by deep breaths and is associated with mild shortness of breath. Patient denies fever, cough, or chills, and has no prior history of similar symptoms, but states she takes Aspirin daily for an unknown reason.
physician_note: 22F with obesity presenting with atypical chest pain and dyspnea. Differential includes musculoskeletal pain, GERD, anxiety, and less likely, pulmonary embolism or atypical cardiac event given borderline troponin. Initial workup shows borderline troponin and mild leukocytosis. Plan: Continue cardiac monitoring, repeat troponin, chest X-ray, and consider GI cocktail. Cardiology consultation if troponin trend increases.

Name: John BELl
chief_complaint: Worsening joint pain and swelling, not improving
hpi: A 17-year-old male with a history of juvenile idiopathic arthritis presents to the ED with a 3-day history of worsening polyarticular joint pain and swelling, primarily affecting his bilateral knees and wrists. He reports increased stiffness and limited range of motion despite regular ibuprofen use. The patient denies fever, rash, or recent illness, but notes generalized fatigue.
physician_note: A&P: 17 y.o. male with known JIA presenting with an acute exacerbation. Vitals are stable, but labs reveal leukocytosis, suggestive of an inflammatory process or possible superimposed infection. Differential includes acute JIA flare, septic arthritis, or reactive arthritis. Plan: Administer IV fluids and analgesia, obtain inflammatory markers (ESR/CRP), consider joint aspirate from the most symptomatic joint, and consult rheumatology for further management and potential systemic corticosteroid therapy.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_2bf28b79
chief_complaint: Sudden right-sided weakness and difficulty speaking
hpi: A 74-year-old female with a history of hypertension on aspirin presents with acute onset right-sided weakness, slurred speech, and facial droop. Symptoms began approximately 90 minutes ago while she was eating breakfast. She denies head trauma, chest pain, or shortness of breath. Last known well was 90 minutes prior to symptom onset.
physician_note: 74F presenting with acute neurological deficits concerning for ischemic stroke. Differential includes acute ischemic stroke, transient ischemic attack (TIA), or intracranial hemorrhage. Plan: STAT head CT, stroke protocol activation, neurology consult, labs, and monitor neuro status closely. Consider thrombolytic therapy if indicated and within window.

Name: shAnnOn BOyD
chief_complaint: Feeling tired and weak for days
hpi: A 52-year-old male with a history of type 2 diabetes mellitus and coronary artery disease presents with a 3-day history of generalized weakness and fatigue. He denies fever, chills, chest pain, shortness of breath, or abdominal pain. Patient reports stable home blood glucose readings and adherence to his medication regimen, including aspirin.
physician_note: Assessment: 52 y/o male with DM2 and CAD presenting with non-specific weakness and fatigue. Vitals are stable, and initial labs are reassuring. Differential includes mild viral syndrome, mild dehydration, or a non-cardiac etiology for his symptoms. Plan: Continue supportive care with oral hydration. Review home medications. Will discharge home with strict return precautions and follow-up with PCP for further workup if symptoms persist.

Name: jAMES GARCiA
chief_complaint: Bad stomach pain and feeling weak
hpi: 53-year-old female with a history of arthritis, on chronic ibuprofen, presents with 2 days of worsening epigastric abdominal pain. She reports associated black, tarry stools and generalized weakness. Patient denies vomiting, fever, or chest pain, but notes increased fatigue over the past week.
physician_note: A/P: 53F with symptoms concerning for acute upper GI bleed, likely NSAID-induced gastritis or ulcer given ibuprofen use and melena. Differential includes pancreatitis, ischemic colitis. Plan: Initiate IV fluids, start high-dose PPI, obtain type and cross, complete CBC, CMP, and serial troponins. Consult GI for possible urgent endoscopy and admit patient for further workup and close observation.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_85f91744
chief_complaint: Sudden worst headache of my life, with vomiting
hpi: A 68-year-old male with a history of hypertension and on aspirin presents to the ED with sudden onset of the "worst headache of his life" approximately 2 hours prior to arrival. The headache is associated with projectile vomiting and rapidly progressing altered mental status. Family reports he became lethargic and disoriented and is now difficult to arouse.
physician_note: A&P: 68 y.o. male presenting with acute onset severe headache, vomiting, and altered mental status, highly concerning for hemorrhagic stroke, likely intraparenchymal hemorrhage given sudden onset and rapid decline. Differential includes subarachnoid hemorrhage, ischemic stroke with hemorrhagic conversion, or mass lesion. Plan: Emergent head CT, stat neurosurgery consult, obtain comprehensive labs including coagulation panel, initiate supportive care including airway protection if needed, strict blood pressure management.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_ab72ee92
chief_complaint: Routine blood pressure check
hpi: This 38-year-old male presents for a routine follow-up for his hypertension. He reports adherence to his Lipitor medication and denies any chest pain, shortness of breath, dizziness, or other acute concerns. He feels well and has no new symptoms since his last visit.
physician_note: Patient presents for routine HTN follow-up, well-controlled on current medications with no acute complaints. Vitals are stable and within normal limits. Plan is to continue current medication regimen, reinforce lifestyle modifications, and arrange for follow-up with his primary care physician for ongoing management. No acute interventions required today.

Name: joy CHAveZ
chief_complaint: Chest pressure and lightheadedness
hpi: A 47-year-old female with a history of hypertension and daily aspirin use presents with 6 hours of intermittent, dull substernal chest pressure radiating to her left arm, accompanied by lightheadedness. physician_note: A&P: 47F presenting with chest pain and lightheadedness, concerning for acute coronary syndrome given risk factors and symptoms. Differential includes unstable angina, NSTEMI, and non-cardiac etiologies such as GERD or anxiety. Initial workup including ECG showed non-specific T-wave changes. Plan: Continue cardiac monitoring, administer aspirin 325mg chewable, draw serial troponins and other cardiac enzymes, and obtain a chest X-ray. Will admit for further evaluation and management.

Name: Maurice steelE
chief_complaint: Bad knee pain, swollen and hot
hpi: A 59-year-old male with a history of osteoarthritis and on Lipitor presents with a two-day history of worsening right knee pain, swelling, and warmth. He reports subjective fevers and chills, but denies any recent trauma or injury to the joint. His pain is now 8/10, significantly worse than his usual arthritis flares.
physician_note: Pt presents with acute monoarticular arthritis concerning for septic arthritis given systemic symptoms and rapid progression. Differential also includes acute gout or pseudogout flare. Plan includes STAT right knee arthrocentesis for synovial fluid analysis and cultures, empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics, and pain management.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_6d2c2242

hpi: 65-year-old male with history of osteoarthritis presents with 2 days of worsening right knee pain, swelling, and redness. He reports associated subjective fevers and chills. Patient is unable to bear weight on the affected leg. Denies recent trauma but notes chronic knee pain.
physician_note: Assessment: 65M with acute monoarticular arthritis concerning for septic joint, given fever, erythema, swelling, and inability to bear weight. Differential includes gout, pseudogout, and severe cellulitis. Plan: Right knee arthrocentesis for fluid analysis, IV vancomycin (due to penicillin allergy), pain control, and admission for further management.

Name: STEPhEn Tapia

physician_note: A&P: 20 y/o M with T1DM presenting with classic symptoms consistent with diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA). Vitals indicate tachycardia and tachypnea, and bedside glucose is significantly elevated. DDx includes DKA, severe hyperglycemia without ketosis, or acute infection exacerbating diabetes. Plan: Initiate aggressive IV fluid resuscitation, start insulin drip, frequent electrolyte monitoring, obtain comprehensive metabolic panel, and urine studies.

Name: dAnIEL OcONnOR

physician_note: 75 y/o male with PMH of DM presenting with generalized weakness and lightheadedness. Differential includes dehydration, hyperglycemia, minor infection, or medication-related side effects. Plan includes labs (CBC, BMP, UA), EKG, and IV fluids. Name: PEGgY RoBinSON
chief_complaint: Feeling tired and weak for days

physician_note: Patient is a 74-year-old obese male presenting with subacute fatigue without clear acute findings. Differential includes mild dehydration, medication side effect, or viral prodrome. Plan includes basic metabolic panel, CBC, EKG, and IV fluids for rehydration.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_90b621c2
chief_complaint: Took too many Tylenol pills
hpi: A 34-year-old female presents to the emergency department after intentionally ingesting approximately 30 tablets of 500mg acetaminophen (total 15g) four hours prior to arrival, reportedly due to suicidal ideation. She denies other co-ingestants or alcohol use. Patient has a history of asthma but denies current respiratory distress, and is currently asymptomatic but admits to feeling depressed.
physician_note: A: 34F with acute acetaminophen overdose, potential for acute liver injury. Suicidal ideation is a significant concern requiring immediate psychiatric evaluation. Differential includes other toxic ingestions or underlying medical conditions contributing to symptoms. P: Initiate N-acetylcysteine protocol (NAC) per nomogram immediately. Obtain serum acetaminophen level, LFTs, INR, glucose, and electrolytes. Consult psychiatry for evaluation of suicidal ideation and disposition planning. Monitor vital signs and clinical status closely.

Name: mElIsSA FlEtchER
chief_complaint: Feeling very weak and lightheaded
hpi: 54-year-old female with a history of type 2 diabetes mellitus and hyperlipidemia on Lipitor presents with generalized weakness and lightheadedness for the past 12 hours. She denies chest pain, shortness of breath, or focal neurological deficits. Patient reports inconsistent blood glucose monitoring recently.
physician_note: A: 54yo female with T2DM presenting with generalized weakness, likely multifactorial including potential for suboptimal glycemic control or dehydration. DDx includes hypoglycemia, hyperglycemia, orthostatic hypotension, or mild infection. P: Will obtain comprehensive metabolic panel, CBC, and urinalysis. Administer 1L IV normal saline. Monitor blood glucose and vital signs closely. If labs are reassuring, consider discharge with strict return precautions and diabetes education.

Name: JULiE sTEELE
chief_complaint: Generalized weakness and fatigue
hpi: A 78-year-old male with a history of prostate cancer, currently on active surveillance, presents with generalized weakness and increased fatigue over the past 3 days. Patient states his usual energy levels have significantly declined.
physician_note: Assessment: 78 y/o male with cancer presenting with new onset generalized weakness. Differential includes dehydration, anemia, electrolyte disturbance, or cancer-related fatigue. Given stable vitals and initial normal lab results, acute life-threatening causes are less likely. Plan: IV fluid bolus, repeat basic labs, consult with patient's oncology team for further guidance and outpatient workup, consider discharge if symptoms improve with hydration.

Name: EmILY sImmoNS

hpi: This 15-year-old male with a history of juvenile idiopathic arthritis, currently on aspirin, presents with acute onset of severe right knee pain, swelling, and subjective fever for the past 24 hours. Differential includes acute JIA flare, reactive arthritis, or crystalline arthropathy. Plan includes immediate arthrocentesis for synovial fluid analysis, blood cultures, and initiation of empiric IV antibiotics (e.g., vancomycin and ceftriaxone) after cultures are drawn. Pain control with IV analgesia will be provided. Close monitoring of vitals and serial exams are essential.

Name: mArK dAVIs

physician_note: 80yo male presenting with acute asthma exacerbation. Differential includes pneumonia, CHF, or PE given age and acute onset. Patient is in moderate respiratory distress with diffuse wheezing. Plan is for bronchodilators, steroids, chest X-ray, and labs to rule out infection or cardiac etiology. Will monitor closely in ED.

Name: DenisE rOdRIGuEZ

physician_note: A/P: 14 y/o female with known hypertension presenting with chronic, mild headache. Vitals stable, neurological exam normal. Further workup will be done inpatient as part of her elective admission.

Name: mElisSa yanG
chief_complaint: Weakness, dizziness, and black stools
hpi: An 85-year-old male with a history of hypertension presents to the ED with a three-day history of progressive generalized weakness and dizziness. physician_note: 85M with acute weakness, dizziness, and melena concerning for upper GI bleed, likely NSAID-induced given ibuprofen use. Plan includes IV fluid resuscitation, hold all NSAIDs, type and crossmatch, CBC, CMP, coags, serial cardiac enzymes, and urgent GI consultation for endoscopy.

Name: RYan FREnCH

physician_note: A: 48 y/o female with metastatic breast cancer on chemo presenting with febrile neutropenia, likely early sepsis. Ddx includes pneumonia, UTI, line infection. P: Admit to oncology, IV fluids, broad-spectrum antibiotics (e.g., cefepime), labs including CBC with differential, blood cultures x2, lactate, UA, CXR. Monitor closely for signs of worsening sepsis.

Name: dAWn chAveZ

physician_note: A&P: 13yo male with ALL on chemotherapy presenting with febrile neutropenia. Differential includes bacterial sepsis, viral infection, and drug-induced fever. Plan includes immediate broad-spectrum antibiotics after pan-cultures, IV fluid resuscitation, and admission for close monitoring and supportive care.

Name: ChriStopHer COHeN

hpi: 67-year-old female with a history of asthma presents to the ED with progressively worsening shortness of breath and wheezing over the past 48 hours. She reports increased cough and chest tightness, not relieved by her usual albuterol inhaler. Denies fever, chills, or chest pain.

Plan includes nebulized bronchodilators (albuterol/ipratropium), systemic corticosteroids, oxygen supplementation, and close monitoring of respiratory status. Name: caMEroN martInez

Name: pHiLLIP denNis

Name: LIsA bENNett

physician_note: A: 28 y.o. male presenting with hypertensive urgency/emergency given severe headache and visual changes in the setting of uncontrolled HTN. Differential includes primary hypertensive crisis, subarachnoid hemorrhage (less likely given no focal neuro deficits but cannot rule out), or posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome (PRES). hpi: 24F with hx of asthma presents to ED after developing generalized hives, facial swelling, and shortness of breath approximately 30 minutes after taking penicillin for a suspected strep throat. physician_note: A 44-year-old male presenting with classic anginal symptoms. Differential includes Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS), pulmonary embolism, and GERD. ECG shows no acute ischemic changes. Initial troponin is negative. Plan: Monitor, repeat ECG and troponin, consider GI cocktail, and cardiology consult if symptoms persist or worsen.

Name: Terry alVARez
chief_complaint: Severe abdominal pain and vomiting
hpi: Patient is a 15-year-old male with a history of obesity presenting with 12 hours of severe, diffuse abdominal pain, initially periumbilical, now migrating to the right lower quadrant. He reports two episodes of non-bloody, non-bilious emesis and denies diarrhea. He has been taking paracetamol at home with minimal relief.
physician_note: 15 y.o. male with obesity presenting with acute RLQ abdominal pain concerning for appendicitis vs. viral gastroenteritis vs. mesenteric adenitis. Physical exam reveals localized tenderness in the RLQ with guarding, but no rebound. Labs are largely unremarkable, supporting a less severe process or early presentation. Plan includes IV fluids, serial abdominal exams, surgical consult if pain progresses, and further imaging (US abdomen) if clinical picture remains unclear.

Name: eRIn gARCIa
chief_complaint: Severe weakness, chills, and fever
hpi: A 64-year-old male with a history of metastatic colon cancer, currently undergoing chemotherapy, presents to the ED with a 24-hour history of severe generalized weakness, subjective fever, and chills. He denies any focal symptoms such as cough, dysuria, or headache. His last chemotherapy infusion was one week prior.
physician_note: This patient presents with suspected febrile neutropenia in the context of recent chemotherapy. Differential diagnoses include sepsis, chemotherapy-induced myelosuppression, or a viral syndrome. Initial management will focus on pan-culturing, immediate broad-spectrum antibiotics, aggressive fluid resuscitation, and close monitoring for signs of septic shock.

Name: CRYstal pAyNe
chief_complaint: Feeling weak and dizzy, some fever
hpi: A 16-year-old female with a history of osteosarcoma currently undergoing chemotherapy presents to the ED complaining of generalized weakness, dizziness, and subjective fevers for the past 24 hours. She reports decreased oral intake and some nausea but no vomiting or diarrhea. Her last chemotherapy cycle was 5 days ago.
physician_note: A&P: 16 y/o F with osteosarcoma s/p chemotherapy presenting with febrile neutropenia symptoms. Differential includes chemotherapy-induced neutropenic sepsis, viral illness, or dehydration. Plan: IV fluids, broad-spectrum antibiotics (e.g., Cefepime + Vancomycin), blood cultures, CBC with differential, CMP, lactate, and likely admission for close monitoring and further workup.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_a0383452
chief_complaint: Routine diabetes check-up
hpi: 44-year-old female with a history of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, well-controlled on insulin, presents for a routine follow-up visit. Patient reports no acute complaints, denies any symptoms of hypo or hyperglycemia, chest pain, shortness of breath, or fever. Her last A1c was reported to be at goal.
physician_note: Assessment: 44F with well-controlled Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, presenting for routine follow-up with no acute concerns. Differential diagnoses include stable T2DM. Plan: Continue current insulin regimen and reinforce dietary and exercise recommendations. Patient advised to follow up with her primary care physician and endocrinologist for ongoing management. No acute emergency department interventions are required at this time.

Name: mrs. maRIa nEwTon
chief_complaint: Feeling very tired and weak for days
hpi: A 33-year-old female with a history of cancer presents with progressive fatigue and generalized weakness over the past week. She reports decreased oral intake and occasional nausea but denies vomiting, fever, chills, or any focal neurological deficits. No recent chemotherapy or radiation.
physician_note: Pt is a 33 y.o. F with known cancer presenting with chronic fatigue and mild malaise. Vitals are stable and physical exam is non-focal. Differential includes anemia, electrolyte imbalance, or progression/recurrence of underlying malignancy. Given stable vitals and normal labs, plan for symptomatic management with IV fluids, antiemetics as needed, and close outpatient follow-up with oncology for further workup.

Name: TAmMY FErGUsON
chief_complaint: Weakness, fever, and feeling generally unwell
hpi: A 57-year-old female with a history of metastatic breast cancer presents to the ED with three days of progressive generalized weakness, fatigue, and subjective fevers and chills. She reports decreased oral intake and has been feeling increasingly lightheaded. She denies chest pain, shortness of breath, or focal neurological deficits.
physician_note: Assessment: 57F with metastatic breast cancer presenting with febrile neutropenia versus sepsis from unknown source. Differential includes chemotherapy-induced myelosuppression, tumor lysis syndrome, or opportunistic infection. Plan: Admit for IV fluids, broad-spectrum antibiotics, blood cultures, and further workup including CBC with differential, CMP, and lactate. Consult oncology.

Name: dANIeLLe MOReno
chief_complaint: Feeling very weak and dizzy, my blood sugar is high.
hpi: A 36-year-old female with a history of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus presents with a 24-hour history of increasing generalized weakness, lightheadedness, and polyuria. She reports her home blood glucose readings have been consistently elevated despite taking her usual insulin. She denies fever, chest pain, or focal neurological deficits.
physician_note: Assessment: 36F with Type 2 DM presenting with severe hyperglycemia and symptoms consistent with hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state (HHS) or pre-ketoacidosis. Initial labs show significantly elevated glucose and mild leukocytosis. Plan: Aggressive IV fluid resuscitation, initiation of insulin drip, and frequent electrolyte/glucose monitoring. Differential includes severe dehydration, acute infection, or medication non-compliance.

Name: sTephanie SChULTZ
chief_complaint: Teenager feeling very weak, dizzy, and sick to stomach
hpi: 15 y/o female with Type 1 Diabetes presents with 3 days of polyuria, polydipsia, and fatigue. She reports increasing nausea and diffuse abdominal pain for the last 24 hours, with decreased oral intake and occasional vomiting. Her parents note she has been more lethargic and irritable today. She denies fever or recent illness prior to symptom onset.
physician_note: Assessment: 15 y/o female with T1DM presenting with symptoms concerning for diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) vs. severe hyperglycemia with dehydration, possibly triggered by an infection. Differential also includes acute gastroenteritis or appendicitis given abdominal pain. Plan: Initiate DKA protocol, including IV fluids and insulin drip. Obtain comprehensive metabolic panel, CBC, urinalysis, and blood cultures. Monitor closely for neurological changes and electrolyte imbalances.

Name: SaRAH hayES
chief_complaint: Shortness of breath and wheezing
hpi: A 15-year-old female with a known history of asthma presents to the emergency department with acute onset shortness of breath and expiratory wheezing, progressively worsening over the last 24 hours. She reports chest tightness and a productive cough, with minimal relief from her prescribed albuterol inhaler. The patient denies recent fever, chills, or exposure to known viral illnesses.
physician_note: 15yo F presents with acute asthma exacerbation, likely triggered by an environmental allergen given the lack of infectious symptoms. Differential diagnoses include viral bronchiolitis, foreign body aspiration, or less likely, early pneumothorax. Plan: Initiate continuous nebulized albuterol and ipratropium, administer oral corticosteroids, obtain a chest X-ray, and closely monitor respiratory status and oxygen saturation.

Name: NathanIEl LewiS
chief_complaint: Feeling weak and dizzy, almost passed out
hpi: 77-year-old female with a history of hypertension presenting with generalized weakness and dizziness for the past 24 hours. She reports feeling lightheaded upon standing and had one episode of near-syncope this morning. She denies chest pain, shortness of breath, or focal neurological deficits. Her daughter notes she has been more lethargic than usual.
physician_note: Elderly female with acute onset weakness and dizziness, likely multifactorial. Differential includes orthostatic hypotension, cardiac arrhythmia, anemia, and possibly a non-ST elevation myocardial infarction given her risk factors. Will order EKG, CBC, CHEM-7, Troponin, and D-dimer. Initiate IV fluids for potential dehydration and monitor vital signs closely. Consult cardiology if EKG or troponin remains concerning.

Name: NaNcY GonZaLes
chief_complaint: Severe belly pain, abnormal test result
hpi: A 44-year-old obese female presents to the ED with acute onset, severe right upper quadrant abdominal pain for the past 24 hours. The pain is constant, rates 8/10, and radiates to her back, exacerbated by movement. She reports associated nausea and one episode of non-bloody, non-bilious emesis. She recently underwent an outpatient workup for chronic dyspepsia, and an abdominal ultrasound performed yesterday was reported as "abnormal," prompting this ED visit. She has been taking ibuprofen with minimal relief.
physician_note: Assessment: 44F presenting with acute, severe RUQ pain, nausea, and vomiting, with a recent abnormal outpatient abdominal ultrasound. Differential diagnosis includes acute cholecystitis, choledocholithiasis, and less likely, pancreatitis or hepatitis. Given her clinical picture and obesity, acute cholecystitis is highly suspected. Plan: Initiate IVF, antiemetics, and broad-spectrum antibiotics. Obtain emergent repeat abdominal ultrasound and consult general surgery for potential cholecystectomy. Labs: CBC, CMP, lipase, LFTs.

Name: PAtriCk sMith
chief_complaint: Weakness, dizziness, and abnormal blood test result
hpi: An 80-year-old male with a history of arthritis presents with generalized weakness and dizziness for the past 24 hours. He reports daily ibuprofen use for chronic pain and notes dark, tarry stools for several days. A recent outpatient complete blood count showed severe anemia, prompting this emergency department evaluation.
physician_note: A/P: 80 y.o. male with symptomatic severe anemia, likely secondary to chronic NSAID-induced gastrointestinal blood loss. Differential includes peptic ulcer disease, diverticulosis, or occult malignancy. Plan: NPO, IV fluids, type and crossmatch for PRBCs, initiate transfusion protocol, STAT GI consultation for endoscopy, and serial hemoglobin monitoring. Admit to critical care for close observation and management.

Name: CHRistINa BARker
chief_complaint: Feeling very weak and tired
hpi: 83-year-old male with a history of metastatic prostate cancer presents with a 3-day history of progressive generalized weakness, fatigue, and reduced oral intake. He reports mild, diffuse body aches, somewhat alleviated by ibuprofen. Denies fever, chills, cough, chest pain, or any focal neurological deficits.
physician_note: A/P: 83 y/o male with metastatic prostate cancer presenting with generalized weakness. Differential includes dehydration, electrolyte imbalance, anemia, or potential progression of malignancy, though no acute red flags present. Patient is alert and oriented, vitals are stable. Plan: Initiate IV fluids, obtain CBC, CMP, and lactate. Monitor response to hydration, consider discharge vs. admission for further workup based on labs and clinical improvement.

Name: heAThEr cOOK
chief_complaint: Can't catch my breath, asthma attack
hpi: A 79-year-old female with a history of asthma presents with acute onset shortness of breath and wheezing, worsening over the last 12 hours. She reports increased cough and tightness in her chest, unrelieved by her usual albuterol inhaler. Patient denies fever, chills, or chest pain, but reports difficulty speaking in full sentences.
physician_note: 79F with acute asthma exacerbation. Patient is in moderate respiratory distress with diffuse wheezing. Differential includes pneumonia, CHF exacerbation, or PE, but clinical picture favors asthma. Plan includes nebulized bronchodilators, systemic corticosteroids, oxygen therapy, and close monitoring in the ED. Will obtain CXR and labs to rule out other etiologies.

Name: RUsSelL FiGuErOa
chief_complaint: Teenage male with diabetes feeling unwell
hpi: A 16-year-old male with a history of Type 1 Diabetes presents to the ED complaining of generalized malaise, mild fatigue, and increased thirst for the past 24 hours.
