physician_note: 16 y.o. male with T1DM presenting with non-specific symptoms concerning for hyperglycemia or early DKA. Physical exam is benign with no signs of dehydration or respiratory distress. Differential includes mild hyperglycemia, viral illness, or early diabetic ketoacidosis. Plan: Check labs including CBC, CMP, VBG. Administer IV fluids and insulin per protocol if indicated by labs.

Name: marK roDRIGUEz
chief_complaint: Severe difficulty breathing and wheezing
hpi: Patient is a 54-year-old female with a history of asthma, presenting with acute onset of severe dyspnea and wheezing. Symptoms began yesterday and have progressively worsened despite using her albuterol inhaler more frequently. She reports associated chest tightness and a non-productive cough, with no fever or chills.
physician_note: Assessment: 54F presenting with acute severe asthma exacerbation, likely triggered by a viral illness or environmental allergen. Differential includes pneumonia, acute bronchitis, and cardiac ischemia given her chest tightness and borderline troponin. Plan: Administer nebulized albuterol and ipratropium, systemic corticosteroids, supplemental oxygen, and consider chest X-ray. Monitor oxygen saturation and respiratory effort closely, and trend cardiac markers.

Name: mrs. MArY RIVaS
chief_complaint: Feeling unwell, dizzy and weak
hpi: 70-year-old male with a history of hypertension presents to the ED complaining of generalized weakness and dizziness for the past 24 hours. He reports lightheadedness upon standing but denies syncope, chest pain, or shortness of breath. He has been taking his prescribed paracetamol for mild body aches.
physician_note: Patient presents with orthostatic symptoms and generalized weakness. Differential includes dehydration, cardiac etiology (e.g., arrhythmia), medication side effect, or a mild infection. Plan includes IV fluid challenge, comprehensive metabolic panel, complete blood count, EKG, and cardiac enzymes to rule out acute coronary syndrome. Will monitor vital signs closely and reassess after fluid administration.

Name: dENiSE GOoDWIN
chief_complaint: Worried about high blood sugar, feeling tired
hpi: 15-year-old male with Type 1 Diabetes presents to the ED with a chief complaint of feeling more fatigued than usual over the past 24 hours. He reports a home glucometer reading of 320 mg/dL earlier this morning, prompting the visit. He denies polyuria, polydipsia, abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, or Kussmaul respirations.
physician_note: A&P: 15 y/o male with T1DM presenting with mild fatigue and reported hyperglycemia. Current blood glucose is 190 mg/dL. No signs of DKA or other acute complications. Differential includes mild hyperglycemia secondary to dietary indiscretion or minor insulin dosing error. Plan: Monitor blood glucose, encourage oral hydration, reinforce sick day management, and discharge home with close follow-up with endocrinology.

Name: gRAce miLleR
chief_complaint: Worst headache of my life
hpi: A 48-year-old female with a history of hypertension presents with a sudden onset, severe, throbbing headache, described as the 'worst headache of her life,' which began approximately 2 hours prior to arrival. She reports associated photophobia, nausea, and mild dizziness. She denies any focal neurological deficits, fever, or recent trauma. She took her usual Paracetamol with no relief.
physician_note: 48F with h/o HTN presenting with acute onset severe headache concerning for subarachnoid hemorrhage vs. hypertensive emergency vs. complicated migraine. Patient is alert and oriented, without focal deficits on initial exam. Plan includes immediate head CT, lab work (CBC, BMP, troponin), EKG, and strict blood pressure management. Will provide symptomatic relief for pain and nausea.

Name: dAVId GOnzaleZ
chief_complaint: Severe shortness of breath, asthma attack
hpi: A 31-year-old female with a history of asthma presents to the ED with progressively worsening shortness of breath, wheezing, and chest tightness for the past 24 hours. She reports her rescue inhaler is no longer providing relief. Patient denies fever, chills, or recent sick contacts, but notes increased environmental allergens.
physician_note: A&P: 31yo F with acute severe asthma exacerbation. Likely triggered by environmental allergens given recent exposure. DDx includes viral bronchitis, pneumonia, or foreign body aspiration (less likely). Plan: Initiate nebulized albuterol and ipratropium, administer systemic corticosteroids, and supplemental oxygen to maintain SpO2 >92%. Obtain CXR to rule out consolidation and CBC. Consider admission if no significant improvement after aggressive initial management. Patient is currently on Lipitor.

Name: johN MonTGOmery
chief_complaint: Generalized weakness and not feeling right for days
hpi: An 85-year-old male with a history of hypertension presents with generalized weakness and fatigue for the past 3 days. He reports feeling lightheaded intermittently and has had a decreased oral intake. He denies chest pain, shortness of breath, fever, or obvious signs of bleeding.
physician_note: Assessment: 85 y.o. male with chronic hypertension presenting with generalized weakness and lightheadedness. Differential includes dehydration, electrolyte derangement, mild infection (e.g., UTI), or a non-ST elevation myocardial infarction (NSTEMI). Plan: Obtain comprehensive metabolic panel, CBC, troponin, and urinalysis. Initiate IV fluids for rehydration. Monitor vital signs closely and consider EKG for cardiac evaluation.

Name: NicOLE MENdeZ
chief_complaint: Sudden chest pressure, not going away
hpi: A 56-year-old female with a history of hypertension presents to the ED with sudden onset substernal chest pressure that started approximately 2 hours ago. The pain is described as a 6/10 pressure, non-radiating, and unrelieved by rest. She denies associated shortness of breath, palpitations, diaphoresis, or nausea. She is currently on Aspirin for primary prevention.
physician_note: A&P: 56 y/o female with HTN presenting with acute chest pain concerning for ACS vs. anxiety vs. musculoskeletal etiology. Initial ECG non-ischemic, initial troponin negative, and vitals are stable. Plan: Continue cardiac workup with serial troponins and repeat ECGs. Administer aspirin and nitroglycerin as needed. Consider imaging if no cardiac source identified. Monitor closely in observation unit.

Name: kaThleen boyD
chief_complaint: Bad headache and blurry vision
hpi: A 15-year-old female with a history of essential hypertension presents to the ED with a severe, throbbing headache, described as 9/10, that started suddenly 6 hours ago. She also reports associated blurry vision and mild photophobia. She admits to inconsistent adherence to her prescribed lisinopril for the past two weeks. Denies fever, focal weakness, or recent head trauma.
physician_note: 15 y.o. female presenting with acute severe headache and visual changes concerning for hypertensive crisis given history of non-compliance. Differential includes hypertensive encephalopathy, severe migraine, or less likely, intracranial hemorrhage. Plan: IV lab work including BMP, CBC, urinalysis. Administer IV labetalol for BP reduction. Obtain stat non-contrast head CT. Consult Neurology if CT abnormal. Admit for BP management and further workup.

Name: EtHaN RobINSON
chief_complaint: Severe abdominal pain, fever, and generalized weakness
hpi: 84-year-old male with history of pancreatic cancer s/p recent chemotherapy presents with 6 hours of worsening, diffuse abdominal pain, associated with chills, subjective fever, and generalized weakness. Denies vomiting or diarrhea. Last chemotherapy cycle was 10 days ago, raising concern for neutropenic fever or acute abdominal process.
physician_note: Elderly male with active cancer and recent chemotherapy presenting with signs of systemic inflammatory response and acute abdomen. Differential includes neutropenic fever with occult infection, bowel perforation, or tumor-related complication. Patient appears acutely ill and requires immediate workup and intervention. Plan includes STAT labs, pan-CT abdomen/pelvis, broad-spectrum antibiotics, IV fluids, and admission to inpatient oncology for further management.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_4d14e0c8
chief_complaint: Severe belly pain after surgery
hpi: A 47-year-old female, s/p gastric bypass several years ago, presents with acute onset severe diffuse abdominal pain for 6 hours. The pain is constant, sharp, rated 9/10, and not relieved by oral paracetamol. She reports associated nausea and one episode of non-bloody, non-bilious emesis. Patient denies fever, chills, diarrhea, or urinary symptoms.
physician_note: 47 y/o F s/p gastric bypass presenting with acute severe abdominal pain, concerning for an internal hernia. Differential diagnoses include bowel obstruction, anastomotic leak, or less likely, gastroenteritis. Labs drawn, IV fluids initiated, and patient made NPO. STAT CT abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast ordered to evaluate for internal hernia or other acute abdominal pathology, and a surgical consult will be placed pending imaging results.

Name: kAylA sMITH
chief_complaint: Weakness and stomach pain
hpi: An 80-year-old male with a history of osteoarthritis, who regularly takes ibuprofen for pain, presents with several days of increasing generalized weakness, fatigue, and mild diffuse abdominal pain. He reports occasional dark stools but denies frank melena or hematochezia. No fever, nausea, vomiting, or recent illness reported.
physician_note: Patient is an 80-year-old male presenting with generalized weakness and abdominal pain, likely related to chronic NSAID use. Differential includes upper GI bleed, acute kidney injury, and electrolyte derangement. Initial labs are partially reassuring but further investigation, including CBC, CMP, and stool occult blood, is warranted. Plan for IV fluids, proton pump inhibitor, and close monitoring.

Name: Amy JeNSEN
chief_complaint: Feeling weak and confused for two days
hpi: 76-year-old female with a history of osteoarthritis presents to the ED with her family reporting generalized weakness and increasing confusion over the past 48 hours. Patient has had decreased oral intake and has been lethargic. No fever or chills reported at home.
physician_note: 76F with acute onset weakness and altered mental status, likely secondary to an infectious process (e.g., UTI, pneumonia) or metabolic derangement (e.g., AKI). Initial labs show leukocytosis, elevated creatinine, and mild lactic acidosis. Plan: IV fluids, obtain blood and urine cultures, start broad-spectrum antibiotics (e.g., ceftriaxone), chest X-ray, and monitor for clinical improvement.

Name: loRI TaYLoR
chief_complaint: Worsening right knee pain and swelling
hpi: 54-year-old male with known osteoarthritis presents with acute on chronic right knee pain, worsening over the past two days. Patient reports increased swelling and stiffness, limiting ambulation. Denies any preceding trauma, fever, chills, or redness of the joint.
physician_note: A: Right knee pain, likely an acute exacerbation of osteoarthritis. Differential includes gouty arthritis or a meniscal tear, with septic arthritis less likely given absence of systemic symptoms. P: Will obtain right knee X-rays to rule out acute fracture or significant effusion. Administer oral NSAIDs for pain control and recommend RICE therapy. Discharge home with follow-up instructed with PCP or orthopedics if symptoms persist or worsen.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_9a383f9d
chief_complaint: Acute shortness of breath and sharp chest pain
hpi: A 44-year-old female with a history of cancer presents to the ED with acute onset dyspnea and sharp, pleuritic chest pain that started an hour ago. She reports no fever or cough. Pain is 8/10 and worsens with deep inspiration, unrelieved by her home paracetamol.
physician_note: Patient is a 44F with known cancer presenting with acute pleuritic chest pain and dyspnea. Initial workup concerning for pulmonary embolism given her history and symptoms. Differential includes PE, pneumonia, pericarditis, musculoskeletal pain. Plan to admit for further evaluation, initiate anticoagulation, and obtain CT pulmonary angiogram.

Name: ryAn trAN
chief_complaint: Severe trouble breathing, asthma attack
hpi: A 39-year-old female with a history of asthma presents to the emergency department with acute onset of shortness of breath, wheezing, and chest tightness for the past three hours. She reports no relief with her home albuterol inhaler. Denies fever, cough with sputum, or recent upper respiratory infection symptoms.
physician_note: Patient presenting with an acute asthma exacerbation, likely moderate to severe based on clinical presentation and persistent hypoxemia. Differential diagnoses include acute bronchitis or allergic reaction. Plan includes aggressive bronchodilator therapy, systemic corticosteroids, close respiratory monitoring, and consideration for chest radiography and arterial blood gas if no rapid improvement.

Name: MARY ColEmaN
chief_complaint: Sudden dizziness and feeling weak
hpi: A 66-year-old obese female with a history of hyperlipidemia on Lipitor presents with acute onset dizziness and generalized weakness starting this morning. Symptoms are worse with standing and improve with rest. Denies chest pain, palpitations, focal neurological deficits, or recent fevers.
physician_note: Patient presents with acute dizziness and weakness concerning for orthostatic changes, possible transient ischemic attack, or medication effect. Vitals are stable, and initial physical exam is non-focal. Plan includes comprehensive metabolic panel, EKG, head CT if indicated by further exam, and IV fluid hydration.

Name: amy dixoN
chief_complaint: Fever and feeling weak
hpi: 26-year-old male with history of acute myeloid leukemia, currently undergoing chemotherapy, presents to the ED with subjective fever to 102F and generalized malaise for the past 48 hours. He denies specific focal symptoms. Recent outpatient labs were noted as inconclusive regarding infection markers, prompting this urgent evaluation.
physician_note: Patient is a young male with AML on active chemotherapy presenting with febrile neutropenia. Initial workup concerning for early systemic infection given immunosuppressed status. Differential includes bacterial sepsis, viral illness, or drug-induced fever. Plan includes pan-culturing, initiation of broad-spectrum IV antibiotics, aggressive fluid resuscitation, and close monitoring with oncology consult.

Name: kEiTh lIn
chief_complaint: Feeling weak and dizzy all day
hpi: A 34-year-old female with a history of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus presents to the ED complaining of generalized weakness and dizziness for the past 12 hours. She reports feeling lightheaded upon standing and has had decreased oral intake today. She denies chest pain, shortness of breath, fever, or vomiting, but reports mild nausea. She is compliant with her diabetic medications and takes daily aspirin.
physician_note: 34F with T2DM presenting with generalized weakness and orthostatic symptoms likely secondary to mild dehydration and suboptimal glycemic control. No acute cardiac or infectious etiology identified at this time. Differential includes viral illness, medication side effect, or worsening diabetic control. Plan includes IV fluids, bedside glucose check, and continuation of home medications. Recheck vitals and glucose post-fluids, with discharge home if symptoms improve, providing return precautions and instructions to monitor blood sugars closely.

Name: eMILy WilsON
chief_complaint: Extreme tiredness, shortness of breath, and feeling dizzy
hpi: 60-year-old male with a history of pancreatic cancer (currently undergoing chemotherapy) presents to the ED with a 3-day history of progressive generalized weakness, profound fatigue, and exertional dyspnea. He reports decreased oral intake and occasional lightheadedness, especially with ambulation. Denies fever, chest pain, or cough. Patient states he missed his last chemotherapy appointment due to feeling unwell.
physician_note: A&P: 60 yo male with pancreatic cancer presenting with acute on chronic weakness and dyspnea, potentially related to disease progression, chemotherapy side effects (e.g., myelosuppression leading to anemia/infection), or dehydration. Initial workup including CBC, CMP, lactate, and ECG ordered. Will monitor vitals closely, initiate IV fluids, and consider transfusion pending labs. Consult oncology for management.

Name: AndREw garCia
chief_complaint: Severe shortness of breath, cannot catch breath
hpi: 53-year-old female with a history of asthma presents to the ED with acute onset of severe shortness of breath that started approximately 2 hours ago. She reports worsening wheezing and cough, unresponsive to her home albuterol inhaler. Denies fever, chest pain, or recent illness. Patient states this is worse than her usual exacerbations.
physician_note: A: 53 y.o. female with acute asthma exacerbation. Differential includes viral bronchitis, early pneumonia, or reactive airway disease. P: Administer nebulized albuterol/ipratropium, systemic corticosteroids (prednisone), and supplemental oxygen to maintain SpO2 >92%. Monitor respiratory status closely, consider chest X-ray if no improvement or signs of infection. Will reassess in 1 hour.

Name: ANthONy HaRvEY
chief_complaint: Sudden weakness, chills, and fever
hpi: A 68-year-old female with a history of metastatic lung cancer presents to the ED with a 2-day history of progressive generalized weakness, subjective fevers, and chills. She reports poor oral intake and increased lethargy. She denies cough, dyspnea, chest pain, or focal neurological deficits.
physician_note: A: 68F with metastatic lung cancer presenting with suspected febrile neutropenia vs. sepsis. Differential includes pneumonia, UTI, or other infectious etiologies. P: Initiate sepsis protocol with IV fluids, broad-spectrum antibiotics, blood cultures, UA, CXR. Monitor vital signs closely and admit for further management.

Name: jaSON roMerO
chief_complaint: Worsening foot pain and redness
hpi: 26F with T1DM presents with a 3-day history of worsening left foot pain and redness. She notes a small cut on her toe developed a week ago which has progressively become more painful and swollen. Denies fever or chills but reports feeling generally unwell.
physician_note: A&P: 26F with T1DM presenting with presumed cellulitis of the left foot. Vitals are stable, and labs show mild hyperglycemia but no significant leukocytosis. Differential includes osteomyelitis, gout, or superficial thrombophlebitis. Plan: IV antibiotics (e.g., vancomycin, zosyn), elevate foot, wound care, and consult podiatry for further evaluation. Monitor glucose levels closely.

Name: whitNEy DAViS
chief_complaint: Weakness and lightheadedness for two days
hpi: 38-year-old male with a history of Diabetes Mellitus presents with two days of generalized weakness and lightheadedness. Patient denies chest pain, shortness of breath, fever, or focal neurological deficits. He reports feeling fatigued and has had a decreased oral intake recently, attributing it to increased work stress. He states he has been compliant with his diabetic medications and daily aspirin.
physician_note: A: Diabetic patient presenting with generalized weakness and lightheadedness, likely secondary to mild dehydration or poorly controlled blood glucose, though symptoms are nonspecific. Differential includes viral illness or mild electrolyte imbalance. P: IV fluids administered, point-of-care glucose obtained, and comprehensive metabolic panel drawn. Patient will be monitored for symptom improvement and lab results. Discharge planning will include dietary counseling and emphasis on regular fluid intake.

Name: HEaTHEr rEeD
chief_complaint: Severe headache, blurred vision, high blood pressure
hpi: A 15-year-old male with a known history of hypertension presents to the ED with a severe headache, described as the worst of his life, accompanied by blurry vision for the past 3 hours. He reports taking ibuprofen for the headache with no relief. He denies fevers, chills, or recent trauma, but expresses concern over the intensity of his symptoms.
physician_note: 15 y/o male presenting with hypertensive emergency with signs of end-organ involvement (severe headache, blurred vision). Differentials include primary essential hypertension with poor control, secondary hypertension (e.g., renovascular, pheochromocytoma), or hypertensive encephalopathy. Plan includes aggressive blood pressure control with IV agents, emergent CT head to rule out intracranial pathology, comprehensive lab workup, and cardiology/nephrology consultation for further evaluation.

Name: jEAn gRavES
chief_complaint: Generalized weakness and fatigue
hpi: 80-year-old male with a history of hypertension presents with a chief complaint of generalized weakness and fatigue for the past 2 days. He denies any chest pain, shortness of breath, fever, or chills. Patient is scheduled for an elective cataract surgery tomorrow and reports feeling more tired than usual which prompted this visit. His hypertension is reportedly well-controlled with home medications.
physician_note: A: Generalized weakness, likely multifactorial. Differentials include mild dehydration, viral syndrome, or mild exacerbation of chronic conditions. P: Initiate IV fluids for hydration, obtain routine labs including CBC, CMP, and EKG. Observe for clinical improvement and re-evaluate prior to discharge, potentially rescheduling elective surgery if symptoms persist.

Name: jeSsica ANderSON
chief_complaint: Sudden severe right lower abdominal pain
hpi: A 21-year-old obese male presents to the ED with sudden onset, severe right lower quadrant abdominal pain for the past 12 hours. Pain is constant, sharp, and rates 8/10, radiating to his back. He reports associated nausea, one episode of non-bloody, non-bilious emesis, and subjective fevers.
physician_note: 21 y/o obese male with acute RLQ pain concerning for appendicitis. Differential includes mesenteric adenitis or complicated diverticulitis. Plan includes STAT abdominal CT with contrast, CBC, CMP, urinalysis, IV fluids, and pain control. Surgical consult will be placed pending imaging results.

Name: alExAndeR SHannON
chief_complaint: Severe headache, blurry vision
hpi: A 16-year-old male with a history of hypertension presents to the ED reporting a sudden onset, severe headache this morning, rated 8/10. He describes associated blurry vision and mild nausea. He denies fever, neck stiffness, or focal neurological deficits. He took his usual paracetamol without relief.
physician_note: 16 y/o M with known HTN presenting with acute severe headache and visual changes, concerning for hypertensive urgency/emergency or possible intracranial pathology. Differential includes migraine or secondary headache. Plan: Obtain IV access, STAT labs including CBC, BMP. EKG. Head CT without contrast. Initiate oral anti-hypertensive if indicated.

Name: jIm DAY
chief_complaint: Bad stomach pain and feeling dizzy
hpi: A 14-year-old male with a history of juvenile idiopathic arthritis, maintained on Aspirin, presents with 24 hours of severe, constant epigastric pain. He reports associated nausea, decreased oral intake, and lightheadedness upon standing. Denies fever, vomiting, or melena currently, but has experienced intermittent heartburn for several weeks.
physician_note: A&P: 14 y/o M with JIA on daily Aspirin presenting with acute severe epigastric pain, likely secondary to aspirin-induced gastritis/ulceration, possibly with mild dehydration. DDx includes pancreatitis, cholecystitis, or early appendicitis. Plan: IV fluids, antiemetics, pain control, PPI, and labs including CBC, CMP, lipase. Close observation.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_e00e71f4
chief_complaint: Vague chest discomfort, nausea, and fatigue
hpi: 79-year-old female with a history of hypertension presents with several days of vague, non-exertional chest discomfort, intermittent nausea, and increasing fatigue. She reports taking aspirin daily. The patient denies diaphoresis, shortness of breath, or radiation of pain, but states she 'just doesn't feel right.'
physician_note: A&P: 79yo female with atypical chest pain symptoms concerning for an acute coronary syndrome given age, comorbidities (HTN), and non-specific presentation. Differential diagnosis includes ACS, GERD, anxiety, and musculoskeletal pain. Plan: ECG, serial troponins, CXR, comprehensive metabolic panel, CBC, IV access, cardiac monitoring, and aspirin 325mg chew.

Name: erICa pATteRsOn
chief_complaint: Weakness, confusion, and stomach pain
hpi: A 22-year-old male with a history of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus presents to the ED complaining of generalized weakness, confusion, and diffuse abdominal pain for the past 12 hours. He reports poor oral intake and missed insulin doses due to nausea and malaise. His family notes increasing lethargy and difficulty rousing him over the last several hours.
physician_note: 22 y.o. male with T2DM presenting with symptoms highly suggestive of Diabetic Ketoacidosis. Differential diagnoses include DKA precipitated by infection, severe gastroenteritis, or metabolic encephalopathy. Plan: IV fluids, insulin drip initiation, serial chemistries, complete blood count, urinalysis, and blood cultures to rule out infectious etiology. Close monitoring of glucose and electrolytes.

Name: johN bLanCHarD
chief_complaint: Generalized weakness and stomach discomfort
hpi: Patient is a 58-year-old male with a history of osteoarthritis, on daily aspirin. He presents with 2 days of generalized weakness, fatigue, and mild, intermittent epigastric discomfort, not clearly reproducible. Denies fever, chills, syncope, or significant bleeding. Aspirin dosage unchanged.
physician_note: A&P: 58-year-old male presenting with generalized weakness and epigastric discomfort. Initial workup, including EKG and labs, shows a borderline troponin, necessitating serial evaluation. Differential includes gastritis, atypical acute coronary syndrome, or possibly a viral syndrome. Plan: Admit for observation, serial troponins, symptomatic management, and further cardiac workup or GI evaluation as indicated.

Name: JUan klEiN
chief_complaint: Worsening joint pain and general malaise
hpi: A 58-year-old female with a known history of severe arthritis, currently on Ibuprofen, presents to the ED with a 3-day history of progressively worsening generalized joint pain, significantly impacting her mobility and sleep. She reports associated fatigue and occasional low-grade fevers at home, despite regular ibuprofen use. She denies any new trauma, swelling, or focal redness, but feels generally unwell.
physician_note: 58F presenting with acute on chronic arthritis pain and systemic symptoms. Differential includes acute inflammatory flare, atypical infection, or medication-related issues. Plan includes CBC, ESR, CRP, renal function panel, urinalysis, and plain films of the most affected joints. Initiate IV fluids for hydration and provide aggressive pain control. Consult rheumatology if labs suggest systemic inflammatory process.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_9b245932
chief_complaint: Injuries after high-speed car crash
hpi: A 23-year-old male presents to the ED after a high-speed motor vehicle collision. Patient was extricated from the vehicle at the scene by EMS, who reported an initial GCS of 12 (E3V4M5) and significant mechanism of injury. En route, the patient became hypotensive with a blood pressure of 88/52 mmHg. A point-of-care FAST exam in the ED was positive for free fluid in the abdomen.
physician_note: 23M with polytrauma following a high-speed MVC, presenting with altered mental status (GCS 12), hypotension, and a positive FAST exam highly concerning for significant intra-abdominal hemorrhage. Differential includes splenic laceration, liver injury, or major retroperitoneal bleed. Plan: Full trauma team activation, massive transfusion protocol initiation, emergent pan-CT imaging, and immediate surgical consultation for potential laparotomy.

Name: amANda BEcK
chief_complaint: Fever and feeling weak after chemo
hpi: A 23-year-old female with a history of acute lymphoblastic leukemia, currently receiving chemotherapy, presents with a 24-hour history of subjective fever and generalized weakness. She endorses chills and poor appetite but denies focal pain, cough, or dysuria. Her last chemotherapy cycle was 5 days prior.
physician_note: A: 23 y/o F with ALL on chemo presenting with presumed febrile neutropenia. Differential includes early sepsis of unknown origin, drug-induced fever, or viral syndrome. P: Admit to oncology service for close monitoring. Initiate blood cultures, urinalysis, and broad-spectrum IV antibiotics immediately. Continue supportive care with IV fluids and antipyretics.

Name: nATHaN marTIn
chief_complaint: Worst headache ever, blurry vision
hpi: A 26-year-old female with a history of hypertension and on aspirin presents to the ED with a severe, throbbing headache for the past 12 hours, accompanied by new-onset blurry vision. She reports non-compliance with her home antihypertensive medications for the last week. Denies fever, focal weakness, or neck stiffness.
physician_note: Assessment: 26 y.o. female with hypertensive urgency presenting with headache and visual changes. Differential diagnoses include pre-eclampsia (even if not currently pregnant, given age and HTN history), posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome (PRES), complicated migraine, or subarachnoid hemorrhage. Plan: Initiate IV antihypertensive therapy (Labetalol), stat head CT without contrast, EKG, and comprehensive lab panel. Strict BP control and neurology consult pending imaging results.

Name: CHrISTophER ShaH
chief_complaint: Sudden chest discomfort and feeling unwell
hpi: 79 y/o male with history of hypertension presents with sudden onset of substernal chest discomfort described as pressure, radiating to the left arm, for the past 2 hours. Associated with generalized weakness and mild dyspnea. He denies fever or cough. Patient took Paracetamol at home with no relief.
physician_note: A&P: 79 y/o male with new onset chest pain concerning for acute coronary syndrome (ACS). Differentials include unstable angina, NSTEMI, or atypical presentation of esophageal spasm. Plan: Stat ECG, serial troponins, CXR, IV access, cardiac monitoring, aspirin, and nitrate administration. Cardiology consult will be placed.

Name: chelsEa FInLeY
chief_complaint: Acute right lower quadrant abdominal pain
hpi: A 17-year-old female presents with acute onset right lower quadrant abdominal pain for the past 6 hours, described as sharp and constant, 7/10 in intensity. She denies associated fever, nausea, vomiting, or changes in bowel habits. She reports taking ibuprofen at home with minimal relief prior to arrival.
physician_note: A 17-year-old female presents with acute right lower quadrant abdominal pain. Differential diagnosis includes appendicitis, ovarian pathology, UTI, and musculoskeletal strain. Physical exam is limited by body habitus and tenderness. Plan includes obtaining a urine pregnancy test, CBC, and urinalysis. Will consider abdominal ultrasound to further evaluate potential appendiceal or gynecological etiologies. Pain management initiated.

Name: mIchaEla RobeRTs
chief_complaint: Severe right upper quadrant pain with nausea and vomiting
hpi: A 30-year-old obese female presents with acute onset, severe right upper quadrant abdominal pain for the past 6 hours, radiating to her back. She reports associated nausea, vomiting once, and denies fever or chills. She has taken Ibuprofen without relief. She notes similar, milder episodes in the past that resolved spontaneously.
physician_note: 30yo obese female presenting with severe RUQ pain concerning for acute cholecystitis. Initial workup shows leukocytosis. Differential includes biliary colic, acute pancreatitis, and gastritis. Plan is IV fluids, antiemetics, pain control, labs (including LFTs, lipase, CBC), and abdominal ultrasound to assess for gallstones and inflammation. Surgical consult will be considered if ultrasound confirms acute cholecystitis.

Name: JEFfrey GIlbErT
chief_complaint: Fever, chills, and generalized weakness for two days
hpi: A 63-year-old male with a history of lung cancer currently undergoing chemotherapy presents with two days of subjective fever, chills, and increasing generalized weakness. He denies cough, dysuria, or focal pain. Patient reports taking paracetamol without significant relief.
physician_note: A&P: 63M with lung cancer on chemo presenting with febrile illness concerning for sepsis. Vitals show fever, tachycardia, and mild hypotension. Plan includes pan-culture, broad-spectrum antibiotics, IV fluids, and STAT labs including CBC, CMP, lactate, and blood cultures. Consult oncology for management guidance.

Name: MEGAn gUZMAN
chief_complaint: Confused and very weak
hpi: Patient is a 68-year-old female with a history of type 2 diabetes, brought in by family for acute onset confusion and generalized weakness over the past 12 hours. Family reports poor oral intake and missed insulin doses for the last two days, along with increased lethargy. She has not been recognizing family members this morning.
physician_note: 68 y/o F with DM presenting with altered mental status and weakness, likely secondary to severe hyperglycemia and possible underlying infection. Differential includes DKA/HHS, sepsis, or CVA. Initial labs show severe hyperglycemia, leukocytosis, and elevated lactate. Plan is aggressive IV fluids, insulin drip, broad-spectrum antibiotics after cultures, and head CT to rule out acute neurological event.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_799c9b68
chief_complaint: Feeling really sick, throwing up, can't catch my breath
hpi: A 28-year-old male with Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus on insulin presents with a 2-day history of nausea, vomiting, and polyuria. He reports missing several insulin doses recently. Patient now complains of generalized weakness and deep, rapid breathing.
physician_note: A/P: 28M presenting in diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA), likely secondary to insulin non-adherence. Initial labs significant for severe hyperglycemia and metabolic acidosis. Plan: Aggressive intravenous fluid resuscitation, insulin drip initiation, and electrolyte repletion with close monitoring for cerebral edema. DDx includes DKA, hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state (HHS), and severe gastroenteritis.

Name: VINCenT EAtOn
chief_complaint: Abdominal pain and vomiting with diabetes
hpi: 17 y.o. male with Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus presenting with a 1-day history of severe abdominal pain, nausea, and multiple episodes of emesis. Patient reports poor oral intake and admits to missing insulin doses due to feeling sick. Denies fever, chest pain, or dysuria.
physician_note: Assessment: 17 y.o. male with T1DM presenting with diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) symptoms. Differential includes DKA, gastroenteritis, and appendicitis. Plan: Initiate IV fluids, insulin drip, and obtain labs including VBG, electrolytes, renal function, and urinalysis. Closely monitor glucose and ketone levels.

Name: StEPHaNIe CLARK
chief_complaint: Chest pain and mild shortness of breath
hpi: A 46-year-old male with a history of hypertension and hyperlipidemia presents with acute onset substernal chest pain radiating to the left arm for the past 2 hours. The pain is described as pressure-like, 6/10 at its worst, and is associated with mild shortness of breath. Patient denies fever, cough, recent trauma, or recent strenuous activity.
physician_note: A&P: 46 y/o male with cardiac risk factors presenting with acute chest pain concerning for acute coronary syndrome. Initial ECG shows normal sinus rhythm without acute ischemic changes. Differential diagnosis includes ACS, pericarditis, GERD, and musculoskeletal pain. Plan: Initiate cardiac workup including serial troponins, aspirin, CXR, and continuous cardiac monitoring. Consult Cardiology for further management.

Name: SArA sAncHez
chief_complaint: Severe stomach pain with vomiting and fever
hpi: Patient is a 24 y/o female with a history of obesity presenting with 1-day onset of severe, diffuse abdominal pain, worse in the right lower quadrant. She reports associated nausea, vomiting x3, and subjective fever. She has been taking ibuprofen without relief.
physician_note: A: 24 y/o female with acute abdomen concerning for appendicitis vs. gastroenteritis vs. ovarian pathology. Labs show leukocytosis and mild elevation in lactate. P: IV fluids, antiemetics, broad-spectrum antibiotics, surgical consult for RLQ pain. CT abdomen/pelvis to rule out appendicitis or other acute pathology.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_5e8b2c56
chief_complaint: Extremely high blood pressure reading
hpi: A 58-year-old female with a history of hypertension, currently on Lipitor, presents to the emergency department after a home blood pressure reading of 195/115 mmHg. Patient denies any associated symptoms including headache, chest pain, shortness of breath, vision changes, or neurological deficits. She reports adherence to her current medication regimen.
physician_note: 58F with known essential hypertension presenting with asymptomatic hypertensive urgency. Physical exam reveals no signs of acute end-organ damage. Differential diagnosis includes medication non-adherence, undiagnosed secondary hypertension, or exacerbation of essential hypertension. Plan is to initiate oral antihypertensive therapy, observe for blood pressure reduction, obtain basic labs, and arrange for close follow-up with her primary care physician.

Name: aShLeY MCIntOSh
chief_complaint: Stomach pain and nausea for two days
hpi: A 22-year-old female with a history of obesity presents with generalized abdominal pain, worse in the epigastric region, starting two days ago. She reports associated nausea, but no vomiting, diarrhea, or fever. Pain is intermittent, described as cramping, and not significantly relieved by OTC antacids.
physician_note: A&P: 22F with obesity presenting with acute, non-specific abdominal pain. Differential includes gastritis, early cholecystitis, or urinary tract infection given vague symptoms. Vitals are stable, and physical exam reveals mild epigastric tenderness without rebound or guarding. Plan is to obtain CBC, CMP, lipase, urinalysis, and consider abdominal ultrasound if labs are concerning or symptoms persist. Patient is NPO for now.

Name: DaNa tuCkER
chief_complaint: Severe asthma attack, can't breathe
hpi: 54-year-old male with a history of asthma presents with acute worsening of shortness of breath and wheezing for the past 24 hours. He reports no relief with increased use of his home albuterol inhaler. Denies fever, chills, or chest pain. States he feels increasingly tired from the effort of breathing.
physician_note: A/P: 54 y/o male presenting with acute asthma exacerbation. Differential includes viral bronchitis, pneumonia, or allergic reaction. Patient is in mild respiratory distress but maintaining airway. Plan: Initiate nebulized albuterol/ipratropium, administer oral corticosteroids, monitor oxygen saturation, obtain chest X-ray to rule out pneumonia, and observe in ER for response to treatment.

Name: LiNDA LOVE
chief_complaint: Feeling very weak and lightheaded
hpi: A 70-year-old male with a history of diabetes presents to the ED complaining of generalized weakness and dizziness for the past 2 days. He reports feeling increasingly lethargic and has had decreased oral intake. His blood glucose meter at home showed readings consistently in the high 300s to 400s. He denies chest pain, shortness of breath, or focal neurological deficits.
physician_note: A 70-year-old male with uncontrolled diabetes presenting with generalized weakness and hyperglycemia. Differential includes DKA/HHS, infection, or medication non-compliance. Plan: IV fluids, insulin drip, labs including BMP, CBC, VBG, urinalysis. Monitor vitals closely. Consult endocrinology.

Name: JimMY esPArZa
chief_complaint: Severe shortness of breath and wheezing
hpi: 75-year-old female with a history of asthma presents to the ED with acute onset of severe dyspnea, wheezing, and productive cough for the past 6 hours. Patient states her rescue inhaler provided no relief, and she feels significantly worse than her usual exacerbations. She denies fever, chills, or chest pain, but reports increased anxiety due to difficulty breathing.
physician_note: A&P: 75 y/o female with acute severe asthma exacerbation, likely triggered by viral illness or environmental irritant. Patient is in significant respiratory distress with hypoxia. Differential includes pneumonia, acute decompensated heart failure, or pulmonary embolism given age and symptoms. Plan: Initiate nebulized bronchodilators, systemic corticosteroids, oxygen supplementation, and obtain chest X-ray and full respiratory panel. Monitor closely in ED.

Name: tHomAs WILLiAmS
chief_complaint: Worsening asthma exacerbation, trouble breathing
hpi: 41-year-old female with a history of asthma presents to the ED with progressively worsening shortness of breath and wheezing over the past 2 days, largely unresponsive to home albuterol. She reports associated chest tightness and increased work of breathing. Denies fevers or chills, but notes generalized fatigue and has been taking PRN Paracetamol for discomfort.
physician_note: Patient presenting with acute asthma exacerbation. Differential considerations include viral bronchitis or early atypical pneumonia given mild constitutional symptoms. Plan includes serial nebulized bronchodilators, systemic corticosteroids, chest X-ray, and a comprehensive metabolic panel with CBC. Will monitor respiratory status closely and consider admission if no significant improvement.

Name: MIChAEl goMEZ
chief_complaint: Feeling very weak and dizzy for days
hpi: 75-year-old male with a history of prostate cancer, currently undergoing chemotherapy, presents to the ED with generalized weakness and dizziness for the past three days. He reports decreased oral intake due to nausea but denies fever, chills, chest pain, or shortness of breath. Patient states he nearly fell yesterday due to lightheadedness. He has not had any recent chemotherapy infusions.
physician_note: Elderly male with known malignancy and recent chemotherapy presenting with non-specific weakness and dizziness. Assessment is likely dehydration and fatigue secondary to chemotherapy, rule out electrolyte abnormalities or anemia. Plan includes IV fluids, comprehensive metabolic panel, complete blood count, EKG, and close monitoring in the ED. Consider oncology consult if labs are significantly abnormal.

Name: debORaH wArREN DdS
chief_complaint: Weakness and fatigue for two days
hpi: Patient is a 75-year-old male with a history of type 2 diabetes presenting with generalized weakness and profound fatigue for the past two days. He reports decreased oral intake and some lightheadedness upon standing. Denies fever, chest pain, or focal neurological deficits.
physician_note: A/P: 75 y/o male with T2DM presenting with generalized weakness. Vitals are stable, and initial labs are reassuring, though glucose is mildly elevated. Differential includes dehydration, mild infection, or suboptimally controlled diabetes. Plan: IV fluids, close monitoring of blood glucose, and further workup as indicated.

Name: DONNa aLlen
chief_complaint: Severe headache and neck pain
hpi: 31-year-old female with a history of hypertension presents with a severe, throbbing headache for the past two days, accompanied by neck stiffness. She reports taking her usual Paracetamol without significant relief. Denies fever, vision changes, focal weakness, or recent trauma.
physician_note: Assessment: Headache, likely tension-type or benign exertional given current stable vitals and absence of red flags. Differential includes mild hypertensive urgency given history, though current BP not critically elevated. Plan: Administer IV fluids and NSAIDs for symptom control. Monitor blood pressure closely. If symptoms improve and neurological exam remains benign, discharge with advice for close follow-up with PCP for hypertension management.

Name: riTA archER
chief_complaint: Mild abdominal pain, diabetic patient
hpi: 15-year-old male with type 1 diabetes presents to the ED for evaluation of mild, intermittent abdominal discomfort over the past 24 hours. He denies fever, vomiting, diarrhea, or changes in bowel habits. Patient reports consistent insulin use and good glucose monitoring, with no recent hypoglycemic or hyperglycemic episodes.
physician_note: Assessment: 15-year-old male with type 1 diabetes presenting with non-specific abdominal pain. Differential includes gastroparesis, gastroenteritis, or muscular strain. Given stable vitals and normal lab work, acute pathology is unlikely. Plan: Recommend outpatient follow-up with endocrinology for diabetes management review and discuss GI symptoms. Discharge home with return precautions.

Name: jAMES huRst DvM
chief_complaint: Chest pain and feeling weak
hpi: A 55-year-old male with a history of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus and hyperlipidemia presents to the ED with substernal chest pressure radiating to his left arm, onset approximately 2 hours ago. He describes the pain as a dull ache, 7/10 in severity, associated with diaphoresis and mild dyspnea. He denies fever, cough, or recent trauma. His home medication includes Lipitor.
physician_note: A&P: 55 y.o. male presenting with acute onset chest pain concerning for acute coronary syndrome (ACS). Given his cardiac risk factors (DM, hyperlipidemia), ACS must be ruled out immediately. Differentials include esophageal spasm, pericarditis, or musculoskeletal pain. Plan: STAT EKG, cardiac enzymes, IV access, aspirin, nitrates if BP allows, and oxygen as needed. Consult Cardiology if troponins are elevated.

Name: JaMeS WIlLiamS
chief_complaint: Sudden, severe headache, worst of my life
hpi: A 57-year-old male with a history of hypertension presents with the sudden onset of a severe headache, described as the "worst headache of his life." He reports associated photophobia and mild neck stiffness. Denies trauma or recent illness and states ibuprofen provided no relief.
physician_note: Assessment: 57 y/o male with acute onset severe headache, concerning for subarachnoid hemorrhage vs. hypertensive urgency/emergency vs. complicated migraine. Plan: Immediate CT head without contrast, stat labs including CBC, CMP, and high-sensitivity troponin. Initiate IV antihypertensive therapy to reduce blood pressure, and consult neurology for further management. Patient will be admitted for observation and workup.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_f550223e
chief_complaint: Routine well-child checkup and asthma follow-up
hpi: This 5-year-old male presents for a routine well-child checkup. His mother reports his asthma has been well-controlled on his current medication, with no recent exacerbations, wheezing, or shortness of breath. He is active, eating well, and has no other acute complaints.
physician_note: Patient is a 5-year-old male presenting for a routine well-child visit. Assessment reveals well-controlled asthma with no signs of acute respiratory distress. Differential includes routine health maintenance. Plan is to continue current asthma management, recommend age-appropriate immunizations, and provide anticipatory guidance. No acute ER interventions required.

Name: kennEth LOPeZ
chief_complaint: Feeling very weak and tired for a few days
hpi: A 72-year-old female with a history of type 2 diabetes presents with a 3-day history of increasing generalized weakness and fatigue. She reports feeling lightheaded on standing but denies syncope, chest pain, or shortness of breath. Her home blood glucose readings have been stable, and she has been taking her prescribed paracetamol for mild body aches, which have not improved.
physician_note: A: 72yo F with T2DM presenting with generalized weakness and fatigue. Differential includes electrolyte imbalance, mild dehydration, urinary tract infection, or atypical presentation of cardiac ischemia given age and comorbidities. P: Labs sent including CBC, CMP, UA, troponin. IV fluids started, EKG obtained. Will monitor vitals and reassess after lab results.

Name: diane Leon
chief_complaint: Generalized aches and mild joint stiffness
hpi: A 17-year-old female with a history of Juvenile Idiopathic Arthritis (JIA) and on Lipitor presents with a 2-day history of generalized muscle aches and mild joint stiffness, particularly in her knees and hands. She denies any fever, rash, or new swelling, and reports good adherence to her current medications. Her symptoms are mild and she is seeking reassurance prior to a scheduled elective follow-up for her JIA.
physician_note: A&P: 17F with JIA presenting with mild, non-specific musculoskeletal discomfort. Differential includes a minor JIA flare, viral syndrome, or mild myalgia potentially related to Lipitor, though symptoms are mild. Given stable vitals and non-acute presentation, plan is supportive care with NSAIDs, continue current medications, and reinforce follow-up with rheumatology as scheduled. No emergent concerns identified.

Name: tAMmy hilL
chief_complaint: Chest pain and trouble breathing
hpi: 69-year-old female with a history of hypertension presents with 3 hours of substernal chest pressure, non-radiating, 6/10 in severity. She reports mild shortness of breath with exertion and denies syncope or palpitations. Patient states she took her prescribed lisinopril this morning.
physician_note: 69 y/o female with HTN presenting with acute chest pain concerning for ACS. Differential includes non-ST elevation MI, unstable angina, pericarditis, or esophageal spasm. Initial EKG shows non-specific ST changes. Plan to admit for cardiac workup, serial troponins, and cardiology consult. Will start aspirin and nitroglycerin as indicated, monitor closely in the ED.

Name: RobErt wilLIAmsOn
chief_complaint: Sudden severe headache, blurred vision, and dizziness
hpi: A 34-year-old female with a history of hypertension presents with the sudden onset of the worst headache of her life, described as throbbing and global. She reports associated blurred vision and dizziness, starting approximately 2 hours prior to arrival. She has been taking Paracetamol without relief, and denies fever, neck stiffness, or focal weakness. Blood pressure at home was reportedly very high.
physician_note: 34F with hypertensive urgency/emergency presenting with severe headache and visual changes. Differential includes hypertensive encephalopathy, subarachnoid hemorrhage, or stroke. Patient is alert and oriented but appears distressed. Plan includes immediate BP control with IV antihypertensives, STAT head CT, comprehensive lab panel including CBC, CMP, and urinalysis, and neurology consultation.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_de0542a0
chief_complaint: Severe difficulty breathing, can't catch my breath
hpi: A 22-year-old male with a history of asthma presents to the ED with acute onset of severe shortness of breath, progressively worsening over the last 6 hours. He reports using his albuterol inhaler every 1-2 hours without relief and states he feels like he's 'suffocating'. He denies fever or recent illness, but reports increased allergen exposure recently.
physician_note: A/P: 22M presenting in severe respiratory distress, consistent with status asthmaticus refractory to bronchodilator therapy. Differential includes acute asthma exacerbation, foreign body aspiration, or anaphylaxis. Patient is tachypneic, tachycardic, and hypoxic. Plan is aggressive bronchodilator treatment, steroids, magnesium, and close monitoring for impending respiratory failure, with intubation readiness.

Name: anDrew JAMeS
chief_complaint: Sudden chest pain and trouble breathing
hpi: 38-year-old female with a history of obesity and hyperlipidemia (on Lipitor) presents to the ED with acute onset, substernal chest pain radiating to the left arm, associated with dyspnea. Pain started approximately 2 hours prior to arrival and is rated 7/10. She denies recent trauma or fever.
physician_note: A: 38F with acute chest pain concerning for acute coronary syndrome. Differential includes myocardial infarction, pulmonary embolism, GERD, and musculoskeletal pain. P: Labs drawn including troponin, ECG performed, and CXR ordered. Will initiate ASA and nitroglycerin, continuous cardiac monitoring. Plan for serial troponins and cardiology consult based on initial workup.

Name: AmY SmIth
chief_complaint: Chest pain and shortness of breath
hpi: This 64-year-old female with a history of arthritis and hyperlipidemia (on Lipitor) presents with acute onset chest pain radiating to the left arm, associated with dyspnea. Symptoms began approximately 4 hours ago and have been intermittent, partially relieved by rest but worsening with exertion. No fever, cough, or recent trauma. The patient denies any recent illness or sick contacts.
physician_note: A: 64F presenting with acute chest pain and dyspnea. Differential includes acute coronary syndrome (ACS), pulmonary embolism (PE), atypical angina, musculoskeletal pain, and anxiety. Initial ECG was non-diagnostic and chest X-ray was clear. Labs show a borderline troponin level. P: Cardiac workup initiated with serial troponins and cardiology consultation. Admit for observation and further evaluation. Consider D-dimer for PE rule-out if cardiac workup remains negative.

Name: JENNa aNdrEWs
chief_complaint: Severe joint pain and swelling
hpi: A 19-year-old female with a known history of juvenile idiopathic arthritis presents to the ED with a 2-day history of worsening polyarticular joint pain, primarily affecting her bilateral knees and wrists. She reports associated swelling, stiffness, and mild subjective fever at home, but denies any trauma or new exposures. Patient takes Lipitor daily for hyperlipidemia.
physician_note: 19F with known JIA presenting with an acute inflammatory flare. Differential diagnosis includes infectious arthritis versus an exacerbation of her underlying autoimmune condition. Plan is to obtain CBC with differential, ESR/CRP, consider joint aspiration if clinical picture warrants, initiate aggressive pain control, and consult rheumatology.

Name: chRisTOPHEr LIttlE
chief_complaint: Diabetic feeling sick, fever, confused
hpi: Patient is a 51-year-old male with a history of Type 2 Diabetes presenting with 3 days of worsening malaise, nausea, and vomiting. He reports subjective fevers, polyuria, and decreased oral intake. His wife notes he has been more confused and lethargic today.
physician_note: Assessment: 51 y.o. male with T2DM presenting with likely severe sepsis given fever, altered mental status, and abnormal labs (leukocytosis, hyperglycemia, elevated lactate). Differential includes DKA/HHS, acute kidney injury, or other severe infectious etiologies. Plan: Aggressive IV fluid resuscitation, broad-spectrum antibiotics after cultures, obtain UA, chest X-ray, replete electrolytes, consider insulin drip for hyperglycemia. Admit to critical care for close monitoring.

Name: ALlIsOn Ruiz
chief_complaint: Sudden severe left knee pain
hpi: A 63-year-old female with a history of osteoarthritis presents with acute onset severe left knee pain for the past 12 hours. She denies any specific trauma but reports the pain has progressively worsened, limiting her mobility. She is currently on Lipitor for hyperlipidemia and states her usual arthritis pain is well-controlled. No fever or chills reported.
physician_note: A: Acute exacerbation of osteoarthritis vs. possible meniscal injury. Patient presents with significant localized knee pain without signs of systemic infection or major trauma. P: Obtain left knee X-rays to rule out acute fracture or significant effusion. Administer oral analgesics for pain control. Consult orthopedics if X-rays are concerning or for further evaluation of potential soft tissue injury. Discharge with follow-up if imaging benign.

Name: JEFFRey rEEveS
chief_complaint: Feeling unwell and weak for a few days

hpi: A 14-year-old male with a history of asthma presents to the ED with acute onset of severe shortness of breath, cough, and wheezing. physician_note: 14yo M with acute severe asthma exacerbation. Pt presents in moderate respiratory distress with accessory muscle use. Differential includes viral bronchiolitis, atypical pneumonia. Plan: Nebulized albuterol/ipratropium, systemic corticosteroids, continuous SpO2 monitoring, consider magnesium sulfate if no improvement.

Name: leSlIE smith
chief_complaint: Fever, chills, and feeling very tired
hpi: A 17-year-old female with a history of acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) currently undergoing chemotherapy, presents to the ED with a 2-day history of subjective fever, chills, and profound fatigue. She reports decreased oral intake and generalized myalgias since her last chemotherapy session 5 days ago. No cough, dysuria, or focal pain reported.
physician_note: A/P: 17 y/o F with ALL on active chemotherapy presenting with suspected neutropenic fever. Initial labs show borderline leukopenia and mild hypoglycemia, but not overtly septic. Given immunocompromised status and recent chemotherapy, high suspicion for infection. Differential includes febrile neutropenia, viral illness, or chemotherapy-induced side effects. Plan: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics (Cefepime/Vancomycin), pan culture, IV fluids, and admission to oncology for close monitoring and further workup.

Name: cyntHiA SMIth
chief_complaint: Worst headache, blurry vision
hpi: A 40-year-old male with a history of hypertension presents with the acute onset of the worst headache of his life, starting approximately 2 hours prior to arrival. He describes the pain as severe, holocranial, and radiating to the neck, accompanied by blurry vision. Patient denies fever, chills, or recent trauma.
physician_note: This 40-year-old male with HTN presents with a thunderclap headache and visual changes, highly concerning for a primary or secondary hypertensive emergency, possibly complicated by subarachnoid hemorrhage (SAH) or posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome (PRES). Differential diagnoses also include acute angle-closure glaucoma or complicated migraine. Immediate plan includes stat head CT, EKG, comprehensive metabolic panel, CBC, and troponin, with close blood pressure monitoring and management to prevent end-organ damage. Will admit for further workup and observation.

Name: LISa DiXOn
chief_complaint: Severe right knee pain, swelling, and fever
hpi: A 20-year-old male with a history of arthritis presents with a 2-day history of acute onset severe right knee pain, swelling, and redness. He reports associated subjective fevers, chills, and generalized malaise. Denies recent trauma or new medications.
physician_note: This 20 y.o. male with known arthritis presents with acute monoarthritis of the right knee. Differential includes septic arthritis, acute inflammatory flare of underlying arthritis, or crystal-induced arthropathy. Initial labs are equivocal for infection, showing mild leukocytosis. Plan is to perform emergent arthrocentesis for synovial fluid analysis, initiate empiric antibiotics covering common pathogens, and obtain orthopedic consultation.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_3a52887d
chief_complaint: Severe allergic reaction, can't breathe
hpi: A 31-year-old male with a history of asthma presents to the ED after accidentally ingesting peanuts approximately 30 minutes prior to arrival. He reports rapid onset of throat tightness, difficulty swallowing, and generalized itching. He also notes significant wheezing and feeling lightheaded, almost passing out. He has not taken any medications for this reaction prior to arrival.
physician_note: A&P: 31 y.o. male presenting with acute onset anaphylaxis secondary to peanut ingestion, manifesting with respiratory distress (wheezing, throat tightness) and signs of hypoperfusion (hypotension, lightheadedness). Differential includes severe asthma exacerbation, angioedema. Plan: Administer epinephrine IM immediately, initiate IV fluid bolus, administer IV corticosteroids and H1/H2 antihistamines. Continuous cardiac and respiratory monitoring is critical; prepare for potential airway intervention if respiratory status deteriorates. Labs drawn.

Name: DOuGlaS TaYloR

physician_note: Patient is a 57yo F presenting with acute asthma exacerbation. Exam shows diffuse wheezing and accessory muscle use. Differential includes acute bronchitis, pneumonia, or CHF exacerbation, though the history is most consistent with asthma. Plan includes nebulized bronchodilators, systemic corticosteroids, chest X-ray, and CBC. Admission likely if no significant improvement.

Name: roBERT tHoMpsoN

physician_note: This 17-year-old diabetic female presents with stable vitals and non-specific abdominal pain with normal laboratory workup. Patient to follow up with PCP and return for worsening symptoms.

Differential includes hypoglycemia, TIA, dehydration, or potential cardiac etiology given her age and comorbidities. P: Will obtain comprehensive metabolic panel, cardiac enzymes, and EKG. Name: sarAH WaRe

Name: ANDreW walLAcE
chief_complaint: Chest pain, shortness of breath, and sweating

physician_note: Patient presenting with acute chest pain concerning for acute coronary syndrome. Differential includes NSTEMI, unstable angina, esophageal spasm, or anxiety. Name: jULie ANDerSON

Name: kElly fIElDs

Name: naTasha smIth

Name: DENNiS moraLES

Monitor neurological status closely and consider neurology consultation.

Name: ANNe LEONArd

Monitor respiratory status closely, consider chest X-ray to rule out pneumonia if no improvement. Admission likely if no rapid response to treatment.

Name: JonaThan AdaMs

physician_note: Assessment: 65F with cancer on chemotherapy presenting with generalized weakness and fatigue. Differential includes chemotherapy-induced myelosuppression with possible neutropenic fever (despite no reported fever), dehydration, electrolyte imbalance, or disease progression. Plan: Admit for workup of pancytopenia, IV fluids, broad-spectrum antibiotics pending cultures, and close monitoring. Consult oncology.

Name: MaRIE wilLIAms

hpi: A 15-year-old female with Type 1 Diabetes presents with 2 days of worsening abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting. She reports increased thirst and urination, and has been feeling lethargic. physician_note: 15 y/o F with T1DM presenting in DKA. Pt is lethargic but arousable, tachycardic, and tachypneic. Primary differential is DKA, consider appendicitis or severe gastroenteritis given abdominal pain. Plan: IV fluids, insulin drip initiation, serial chemistries, EKG, and close monitoring in ED/ICU.

Name: kELlY DONALDSON
chief_complaint: Severe headache and chest discomfort
hpi: A 72-year-old male with a history of hypertension presents to the ED with sudden onset severe headache, rated 8/10, radiating to the neck, accompanied by chest discomfort for the past 2 hours. He reports feeling generally unwell and lightheaded. He denies fever, cough, or recent trauma.
physician_note: Assessment: 72yo male presenting with hypertensive urgency/emergency with associated chest pain concerning for acute coronary syndrome given elevated troponin. Differential includes acute myocardial infarction, aortic dissection, severe anxiety, or intracranial hemorrhage. Name: LInda hoLT
chief_complaint: Feeling weak and confused, not acting right
hpi: A 76-year-old male with a history of type 2 diabetes presents with acute onset confusion and generalized weakness for the past 24 hours. Family reports decreased oral intake and lethargy. physician_note: A&P: 76 yo male with T2DM presenting with altered mental status, likely secondary to hyperglycemia with possible DKA/HHS or infection. Differential includes stroke, urosepsis, or other metabolic derangement. Plan: IV fluids, obtain full labs including CBC, BMP, VBG, lactate, urinalysis. Initiate empiric antibiotics after cultures if infection suspected. Consult endocrinology for glucose management.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_8ac0c144
chief_complaint: Severe joint pain, arthritis flare-up
hpi: 67-year-old female presents with a 3-day history of worsening polyarticular joint pain, primarily affecting her hands, wrists, and knees. She reports stiffness, swelling, and tenderness, which is significantly interfering with her activities of daily living. She takes Ibuprofen daily for her chronic rheumatoid arthritis but states it is no longer providing adequate relief. No fever, chills, or other systemic symptoms.
physician_note: A: 67F with acute rheumatoid arthritis flare. Differential includes septic arthritis (less likely given afebrile and no systemic signs), gout (less likely given chronic RA and polyarticular presentation). P: Administer IV steroids and pain control. Obtain ESR/CRP and complete metabolic panel. Consult rheumatology for medication adjustment and discharge home with prednisone taper.

Name: heatHeR BeckEr
chief_complaint: Worsening knee pain and stomach discomfort
hpi: 83-year-old male with history of osteoarthritis presenting with a week of progressively worsening bilateral knee pain, unrelieved by ibuprofen. He also reports mild epigastric discomfort and occasional nausea, which he attributes to his chronic ibuprofen use. Denies fever, chills, vomiting, melena, or acute injury.
physician_note: Assessment: Elderly male with chronic osteoarthritis exacerbation and probable NSAID-induced gastritis. Patient is stable on arrival with no acute distress. Differential includes mechanical knee pain, inflammatory arthritis, and dyspepsia. Plan: Discontinue ibuprofen, start PPI, recommend Tylenol for pain, and refer to orthopedics for further evaluation and pain management. Discharge home with return precautions.

Name: MitcHeLl riDDle
chief_complaint: Severe abdominal pain, black stools, and weakness
hpi: 77-year-old male with history of obesity presents with 2-day history of worsening epigastric abdominal pain, described as burning and constant. He reports multiple episodes of dark, tarry stools and increasing generalized weakness and dizziness. Patient takes Ibuprofen daily for chronic knee pain.
physician_note: Patient is an elderly male with acute GI bleed likely secondary to NSAID-induced gastropathy or PUD, given history of Ibuprofen use. Other differentials include diverticular bleed or ischemic colitis. Plan includes NPO, IV fluids, serial Hgb/Hct, type and screen, GI consult for endoscopy, and monitoring for hemodynamic stability. Admit to observation unit.

Name: VicToR CaRLSOn
chief_complaint: Bad knee pain, need medicine
hpi: 60-year-old female with history of obesity and osteoarthritis presents with a 3-day history of worsening left knee pain. The pain is described as a constant ache, rated 5/10, exacerbated by ambulation. She reports taking ibuprofen without adequate relief and denies any recent trauma, fever, or swelling. Patient has an elective total knee arthroplasty scheduled next month.
physician_note: A: Acute exacerbation of chronic left knee osteoarthritis. Differential includes gouty arthritis or possible meniscal tear, though acute infection is less likely given afebrile status and absence of erythema. P: Administered oral analgesics for pain control. Discussed conservative management strategies and advised to follow up with her orthopedic surgeon for pre-operative evaluation. Discharged home in stable condition.

Name: karen milLer
chief_complaint: Severe belly pain, something's not right
hpi: 38-year-old male with a history of recently diagnosed malignancy presents with 2 days of worsening, diffuse abdominal pain. The pain is described as sharp, constant, and rated 8/10, not relieved by acetaminophen. physician_note: 38 y/o male with known cancer presenting with acute, severe abdominal pain concerning for an acute abdomen. Differential includes tumor progression/metastasis, bowel obstruction, perforation, or medication-induced gastritis. Plan includes immediate labs (CBC, CMP, lactate, lipase), upright abdominal X-ray and CT abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast, IV fluids, broad-spectrum antibiotics, and surgical consultation for further management if imaging is concerning for surgical etiology.

Name: TEResa roSE
chief_complaint: Feeling weak and tired for a few days
hpi: A 36-year-old female with a history of type 2 diabetes mellitus presents with generalized weakness and fatigue for the past three days, accompanied by subjective malaise. physician_note: Patient is a 36-year-old diabetic female presenting with generalized weakness and malaise, currently stable on exam. Differential diagnosis includes suboptimally controlled diabetes, viral syndrome, or mild electrolyte disturbance. Given her chronic condition, a comprehensive metabolic panel, CBC, and urinalysis are indicated to rule out acute issues or exacerbation of her diabetes. Will monitor blood glucose and consider IV fluids if hydration status warrants. Discharge planning will focus on diabetes education and close outpatient follow-up once acute concerns are addressed.

Name: paTricK hEbeRT
chief_complaint: Severe headache, blurry vision, and chest pressure
hpi: Patient is a 44-year-old male with a history of hypertension on Lipitor, presenting with acute onset severe headache, blurred vision, and chest pressure for the past 4 hours. He denies fever, cough, or recent trauma. Patient reports compliance with Lipitor but admits to missing his blood pressure medication for several days. He has no prior history of similar symptoms.
physician_note: 44 y/o male with HTN presents with acute headache, blurred vision, and chest pressure. Vitals notable for BP 180/105. No focal neurological deficits or signs of acute end-organ damage on initial assessment. Differential includes hypertensive urgency, migraine with aura, and atypical angina. Plan: Administer oral labetalol, obtain EKG, CBC, CMP, Troponin, and close BP monitoring; will reassess for improvement and stability.

Name: Jesse hAnCOcK
chief_complaint: Feeling weak, confused, and sick
hpi: 76 y/o female with a history of type 2 diabetes presents with a 2-day history of increasing generalized weakness, lethargy, and decreased oral intake. She denies chest pain or shortness of breath.
physician_note: A&P: 76 y/o female with type 2 diabetes presenting with altered mental status, fever, and signs of systemic inflammatory response. High suspicion for sepsis, likely secondary to a urinary tract infection given her age and comorbidities, but also considering DKA/HHS or severe dehydration. Plan: Initiate aggressive IV fluid resuscitation, obtain STAT labs including CBC, CMP, lactate, blood cultures, and urinalysis, and start broad-spectrum antibiotics pending culture results. Patient will be admitted to the ICU for close monitoring and further management.

Name: hARRy CLArk

physician_note: 83 y/o female with HTN presenting with acute on chronic weakness and dizziness. Differential includes dehydration, orthostatic hypotension, cardiac arrhythmia, early sepsis (UTI), or acute coronary syndrome. Initial workup to include comprehensive metabolic panel, complete blood count, troponin, EKG, and urinalysis. Initiate IV fluids and closely monitor vital signs.

Name: suE ShEphErD

physician_note: Elderly female with acute exacerbation of right knee arthritis. Differential includes septic arthritis, gout, pseudogout, and severe osteoarthritis flare. Plan: Right knee X-rays, urgent arthrocentesis for crystal and cell count analysis, initiation of NSAIDs, and joint immobilization. Will admit if septic arthritis confirmed.

Name: deReK VALDEz

physician_note: 44M with known lymphoma on active chemotherapy presenting with febrile neutropenia. Differential includes sepsis, pneumonia, or other opportunistic infection. Plan includes pan-cultures (blood, urine), broad-spectrum IV antibiotics (e.g., cefepime/vancomycin), IV fluids for support, and admission for close monitoring and further workup.

Name: SUsAN BAUEr
chief_complaint: Severe headache, blurry vision, and feeling unwell
hpi: A 26-year-old female with a history of hypertension presents with a sudden onset of severe headache, described as throbbing, 8/10, radiating to the neck, accompanied by blurry vision and mild nausea. physician_note: 26F with known HTN presenting with acute severe headache and visual changes. Differential includes hypertensive urgency/emergency, pre-eclampsia/eclampsia given age, and possible intracranial pathology. Plan: STAT vitals, labs (CBC, CMP, UA, troponin), EKG, head CT, and close BP monitoring. Initiate antihypertensive management as indicated to reduce risk of end-organ damage.

Name: ROBeRT mulLiNs
chief_complaint: Sudden severe right knee pain and swelling
hpi: A 68-year-old male with a history of osteoarthritis presents with an acute onset of severe right knee pain and swelling for the past 24 hours. physician_note: Patient presents with acute monoarticular arthritis, likely an osteoarthritis flare-up, but septic arthritis and gout remain in the differential given the acute severe presentation. Physical exam reveals significant right knee effusion, warmth, and tenderness with limited range of motion. Plan: Obtain right knee x-rays, perform arthrocentesis for fluid analysis (cell count, crystals, culture), and initiate empiric pain management with NSAIDs pending results. Orthopedic consultation to follow.

Name: tAYLOr WARD

physician_note: 70F presenting with stable chest pain, likely stable angina vs. musculoskeletal pain vs. GERD. Vitals are stable, and initial labs, including troponin, are within normal limits. Plan includes EKG, aspirin, and further cardiac workup, potentially a stress test. Patient will be monitored for any changes.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_0f67219a

physician_note: A/P: 29 y/o female presenting with acute suicidal ideation with a specific plan. Differential includes major depressive disorder, adjustment disorder with depressed mood, or substance-induced mood disorder (though denied). Patient is medically stable with no acute medical issues. Plan: Initiate full safety precautions. Obtain urgent psychiatric evaluation. Consult social work for disposition planning to inpatient psychiatric unit.

Name: KathErINE MARtIn

hpi: An 85-year-old female with a history of breast cancer s/p mastectomy and chemotherapy presents to the ED with a chief complaint of generalized fatigue and mild weakness for the past 3 days. physician_note: This 85yo female with cancer presents with mild, non-specific symptoms. Assessment suggests possible cancer-related fatigue or mild deconditioning. Differential includes electrolyte imbalance or early infection, though no overt signs. Plan includes basic labs (CBC, BMP), IV fluids for hydration, and close observation. Will discharge home with instructions for follow-up with oncology if labs are unremarkable.

Name: AsHlEY cArsoN

Name: JaMes BroWn

Labs reveal severe hyperglycemia and mild lactic acidosis. Plan includes aggressive IV fluid resuscitation, insulin drip, and electrolyte repletion. Differential includes DKA, HHS, and severe dehydration.

Name: wanda SmitH
chief_complaint: Chest pain and shortness of breath
hpi: A 59-year-old male with a history of obesity presents to the ED with acute onset chest pain and dyspnea for the past 3 hours. Pain is described as a pressure, non-radiating, 6/10 at its worst, unrelieved by rest. He denies recent trauma or fever but reports feeling lightheaded intermittently.
physician_note: 59 y/o male with obesity presenting with acute chest pain and dyspnea concerning for acute coronary syndrome (ACS). Initial ECG shows non-specific T-wave changes; troponin is borderline elevated at 0.08 ng/ml. Differential diagnoses include ACS, pulmonary embolism, and acute heart failure exacerbation. Plan includes serial troponins, cardiology consultation, D-dimer, CXR, and further workup for dyspnea; strict bed rest and aspirin administered.

Name: pRiSCiLlA dEckEr

hpi: 57-year-old male with a history of metastatic prostate cancer, currently receiving palliative chemotherapy, presents with a chief complaint of progressively worsening fatigue and mild exertional dyspnea over the past 10 days. physician_note: 57 y.o. male with metastatic prostate cancer presenting with chronic fatigue and mild dyspnea. Patient is hemodynamically stable, and initial workup including CBC, CMP, and EKG is unremarkable. Differential includes chemotherapy-induced fatigue, anemia of chronic disease, or possible progression of malignancy. Given stable condition, patient can be safely discharged. Plan: Discharge home with instructions to follow up with oncology within 1-2 days. Consider outpatient iron studies and further anemia workup. Return to ED for any worsening symptoms.

Name: chrIStINA broWn
chief_complaint: Feeling weak and tired for two days
hpi: A 30-year-old male with a history of testicular cancer, currently undergoing chemotherapy, presents with two days of generalized weakness and fatigue. He denies fever, chills, cough, chest pain, or dyspnea. Patient is adherent to his oncology regimen and takes aspirin occasionally for mild headaches.
physician_note: A&P: 30M with known testicular cancer on active chemotherapy presenting with generalized malaise and fatigue, likely related to treatment side effects or potential viral syndrome. Vitals are stable, and physical exam is unremarkable. Differential includes chemotherapy-induced fatigue, mild viral infection, or early neutropenic process. Plan: Obtain CBC with differential, CMP, and consider blood cultures. Provide IV fluids for hydration and consult with oncology for further guidance.

Name: RIcHaRd rObiNSoN
