hpi: An 80-year-old female with obesity presents to the ED after a syncopal episode at home. Patient reports feeling dizzy for the past day, followed by a brief loss of consciousness while standing up from a chair. She denies head trauma but endorsed generalized weakness and lightheadedness prior to the fall.
physician_note: A&P: 80yo F with obesity presenting after syncopal episode. Vitals are stable, physical exam unremarkable aside from mild orthostatic changes. Differential includes orthostatic hypotension, dehydration, vasovagal syncope, or less likely, a cardiac arrhythmia given normal ECG. Plan: IV fluids, orthostatic vital signs monitoring, basic labs, and consider telemetry if symptoms persist. Patient will be discharged once stable with fall precautions education.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_3761c75e
chief_complaint: Feeling very weak, confused, and feverish
hpi: A 58-year-old female with a history of diabetes presents to the ED with altered mental status, generalized weakness, and fever for the past 24 hours. Family reports progressive lethargy, decreased oral intake, and decreased urine output. Patient appears acutely ill and somnolent upon arrival.
physician_note: A: 58 y.o. female presenting in septic shock, likely from an acute infectious process such as urosepsis, given her diabetes and current presentation. Patient is profoundly hypotensive, tachycardic, and febrile with significant lactic acidosis. P: Immediate resuscitation with IV fluids, broad-spectrum antibiotics, and initiation of vasopressors. Further workup to include pan-cultures, urinalysis, and imaging as indicated. Differential includes severe DKA, cardiogenic shock, or other severe systemic infection.

Name: tracY DIaz
chief_complaint: Feeling very tired and chest discomfort
hpi: Patient is a 67 y/o male with history of diabetes presenting with 2 days of increasing fatigue and generalized body aches. He reports intermittent, non-radiating chest discomfort, described as a pressure, not relieved by ibuprofen. He denies fever, cough, or shortness of breath. No recent trauma or strenuous activity.
physician_note: 67 y/o male with diabetes presenting with atypical chest discomfort and fatigue. Differential includes acute coronary syndrome, viral syndrome, or diabetic complication given vague symptoms and inconclusive initial workup. Plan: Continue cardiac workup with serial troponins and ECGs. Monitor glucose given diabetes. Admit for observation and further diagnostics.

Name: jAMEs kenNeDY

hpi: A 29-year-old male with a known history of arthritis presents with acute onset, severe right knee pain for the past 24 hours. He describes the pain as throbbing, rated 8/10, and reports no relief from his usual dose of Ibuprofen. There is no history of recent trauma, fever, or swelling noted.
physician_note: A: 29 y.o. male with chronic arthritis presenting with an acute exacerbation of right knee pain. Ddx: Arthritis flare, possible meniscal pathology, or inflammatory arthropathy. P: Administer oral analgesia, apply ice, consider imaging if pain persists or examination changes, continue current medications with close follow-up.

Name: SUSAN goOdmaN
chief_complaint: Trouble breathing, can't catch my breath
hpi: A 74-year-old male with a history of asthma presents with acute onset of worsening shortness of breath for the past 6 hours. He reports increasing wheezing and cough, which has not improved with his usual albuterol inhaler. He denies fever, chest pain, or recent illness.
physician_note: A&P: Elderly male with acute asthma exacerbation, likely severe, complicated by age and potential cardiac involvement. Differential diagnoses include acute decompensated heart failure, pneumonia, or pulmonary embolism. Plan: Initiate aggressive bronchodilator therapy, systemic steroids, obtain STAT ECG, CXR, and comprehensive lab panel including troponin, BNP, CBC, and metabolic panel. Continuous O2 and close monitoring of respiratory effort and oxygenation.

Name: dAVId bUtler
chief_complaint: Feeling very weak and nauseous, blood sugar is high
hpi: Patient is a 20-year-old female with a history of Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus, presents to the ED with complaints of generalized weakness, nausea, and vomiting for the past 24 hours. She denies fever or chills but has been taking ibuprofen for generalized body aches.
physician_note: Assessment: 20 y.o. female with T1DM presenting with diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) symptoms. Vitals show tachycardia and mild tachypnea. Labs reveal significant hyperglycemia and metabolic acidosis. Plan: Admit to ICU for DKA management including IV fluids, insulin drip, electrolyte replacement, and close monitoring. Differential includes DKA, hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state (HHS), or severe gastroenteritis in a diabetic patient.

Name: EdgeCase_edge_fd9c14d1
chief_complaint: Sudden confusion and not acting right
hpi: 82-year-old male with a history of hypertension, on Lipitor, presents to the ED with new-onset confusion over the past 24 hours. Family reports he is disoriented to place and time, struggling to form complete sentences, and has been more agitated than usual. He is currently on 8 medications, with no recent changes noted by family. No fever, headache, or focal weakness reported.
physician_note: 82yo male presenting with acute delirium, likely multifactorial given age and polypharmacy. Differential includes medication toxicity, urinary tract infection, metabolic disturbance (e.g., hyperglycemia, electrolyte imbalance), and less likely, an acute cerebrovascular event. Initial plan includes comprehensive metabolic panel, complete blood count, urinalysis, TSH, CT head without contrast, and thorough medication reconciliation. Close monitoring for clinical changes and further workup as indicated.

Name: jUAN anDersoN
chief_complaint: Sudden shortness of breath and chest tightness
hpi: 82-year-old female with a history of hypertension, on aspirin, presents with acute onset shortness of breath, worse with exertion, for the past 6 hours. Reports associated mild central chest tightness but denies radiation. No fever or cough.
physician_note: A&P: 82F with acute dyspnea concerning for CHF exacerbation vs NSTEMI vs atypical pneumonia. Elevated troponin and mild leukocytosis noted. Plan: Cardiac workup including serial troponins and ECG, CXR, IV diuresis, and respiratory support as needed. Admit to observation.

Name: MAry NoBlE
chief_complaint: Severe headache, dizziness, and weakness
hpi: A 71-year-old female with a history of hypertension presents to the ED with a severe headache, dizziness, and generalized weakness for the past 24 hours. She reports taking her usual ibuprofen for the headache with minimal relief. Denies fever, chest pain, or focal neurological deficits, but notes mild nausea.
physician_note: A: 71 y.o. female presenting with acute onset headache, dizziness, and weakness. Differentials include hypertensive urgency/emergency, TIA, dehydration, or medication side effect. P: Will obtain STAT EKG, head CT without contrast, comprehensive metabolic panel, and CBC. Initiate IV fluids and closely monitor BP. Consider neurology consult if CT is negative and symptoms persist.

Name: mORgAn biRd
chief_complaint: Sudden, severe right knee pain and swelling
hpi: A 44-year-old male with a history of arthritis presents to the ED with abrupt onset of excruciating right knee pain and associated swelling for the past 6 hours. physician_note: Patient presents with acute monoarticular arthritis. Differential includes acute gouty flare, pseudogout, or septic arthritis. Given stable vitals and normal initial labs, septic arthritis is less likely but not completely ruled out without joint fluid analysis. Plan includes rest, ice, NSAIDs, and consideration for joint aspiration if effusion is significant, along with orthopedic consult for definitive management.

Name: CHeryL CHAMBeRS
chief_complaint: Trouble breathing, asthma acting up
hpi: A 58-year-old male with a history of persistent asthma presents with acute shortness of breath and wheezing for the past 6 hours. He reports increasing cough and tightness in his chest, not relieved by his usual albuterol inhaler. He has been taking his prescribed Aspirin daily.
physician_note: A: Acute asthma exacerbation, likely triggered by environmental factors. Differential includes bronchitis or early pneumonia. P: Initiate nebulized albuterol/ipratropium, systemic corticosteroids, monitor respiratory status. Chest X-ray to rule out pneumonia. Consider discharge home with follow-up if stable after treatment.
